input.prompt:
Fill in the roadmap skeleton at docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml.
Replace every TODO with real content. Do NOT change the YAML structure (phases, steps, keys). Fill in: names, descriptions, acceptance criteria, time estimates, dependencies, and implementation_scope paths.

Goal: Implement objective and behavior node types with alignment evaluation, behavior telemetry, policy enforcement, coherence auditing, and behavior learning loop.

## CRITICAL CONTEXT

Read these files for full context before filling the roadmap:
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/architecture-design.md (full architecture)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/data-models.md (SurrealDB schema)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/component-boundaries.md (module decomposition)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/walking-skeleton.md (implementation sequence + dependency graph)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/test-scenarios.md (all 47 acceptance test scenarios)
- CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md (project conventions)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql (existing schema — read before writing anything that references tables/fields)

## STEP MAPPING FROM WALKING SKELETON

The distill artifacts define 7 skeletons in a dependency graph. Map these into roadmap steps:

### Phase 1: Graph Layer Foundation (schema + CRUD)
- Step 01-01: Schema migrations (0032_objective_table.surql, 0033_behavior_table.surql, 0034_objective_fulltext.surql) + objective CRUD queries (app/src/server/objective/queries.ts)
- Step 01-02: Behavior telemetry collection — behavior CRUD queries + scorer (pure) + exhibits edge (app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts, app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts)  
- Step 01-03: Objective and behavior HTTP routes (app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts, app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts) + route registration in start-server.ts

### Phase 2: Alignment and Policy
- Step 02-01: Intent-objective alignment evaluator — pure cosine similarity + supports edge writer (app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts, extends intent/authorizer.ts)
- Step 02-02: Behavior-based policy enforcement — enrich IntentEvaluationContext with behavior_scores, policy gate queries latest scores (extends policy/types.ts, policy/policy-gate.ts)

### Phase 3: Visibility and Auditing
- Step 03-01: Objective progress visibility — supporting intent count, success criteria tracking (extends objective/queries.ts, objective-route.ts)
- Step 03-02: Coherence auditor — orphaned decisions + stale objectives detection (extends observer/graph-scan.ts)
- Step 03-03: Behavior trend computation (app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts) — pure trend analysis functions

### Phase 4: Learning Loop
- Step 04-01: Behavior learning bridge — feeds behavior trends into existing learning proposal pipeline (extends observer/learning-diagnosis.ts)
- Step 04-02: Graph UI visualization for objective and behavior nodes (app/src/client/ — extend existing graph components, already partially done per git history)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA GUIDANCE

For each step, acceptance criteria MUST:
- Reference specific test scenarios from test-scenarios.md by ID (e.g., "US-OB-01 #1: Leader creates objective")
- Be behavioral and measurable (Given/When/Then or assertion-style)
- Cover the walking skeleton scenario + key focused scenarios for that step

## KEY CONVENTIONS FROM CLAUDE.md

- Development paradigm: functional (pure core / effect shell)
- SurrealDB: SCHEMAFULL tables, no null (use option<T>), RecordId objects everywhere
- Migrations: zero-padded prefix (0032, 0033, 0034), wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION (except DEFINE ANALYZER)
- No backwards compatibility / no data migration scripts
- Step IDs: NN-NN format (01-01, 01-02, etc.)

## FILES_TO_MODIFY

List actual file paths in implementation_scope. New files:
- schema/migrations/0032_objective_table.surql
- schema/migrations/0033_behavior_table.surql
- schema/migrations/0034_objective_fulltext.surql
- app/src/server/objective/queries.ts
- app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts
- app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts

Extended files:
- app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts (route registration)
- app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts (alignment step)
- app/src/server/policy/types.ts (behavior_scores in context)
- app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts (coherence + telemetry scans)
- app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts (behavior trend signal)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql (updated base schema)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-crud.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-telemetry.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/intent-alignment.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-policy.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-progress.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/coherence-auditor.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-learning.test.ts

TASK BOUNDARY: Fill the roadmap skeleton only. Do NOT implement any code. Return control to orchestrator after the roadmap is complete.

roadmap:
  project_id: objective-behavior
  created_at: '2026-03-13T19:13:05Z'
  total_steps: 10
  phases: 4
phases:
- id: '01'
  name: 'Graph Layer Foundation'
  steps:
  - id: 01-01
    name: 'Objective schema + CRUD queries'
    description: >
      Create SurrealDB migrations for objective table, has_objective relation, supports relation,
      and observes OUT extension. Implement objective CRUD query functions (create, get, list, update status).
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: []
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-01 #1: Objective created with title, priority, status, workspace scope and persisted to DB'
      - 'US-OB-01 #2: Objective stores multiple success criteria as key result objects'
      - 'US-OB-01 #4: Objective without target_date persists with field omitted (not null)'
      - 'US-OB-01 #5: Objectives scoped to workspace -- query for workspace A returns zero results from workspace B'
      - 'US-OB-01 #3: List query returns all active objectives within a workspace'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Migration 0032 wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION'
      - 'All fields use option<T> for absence, never null'
      - 'Query functions accept Surreal + RecordId params, return typed results'
      - 'HNSW index on embedding field (DIMENSION 1536, DIST COSINE)'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - schema/migrations/0032_objective_table.surql
        - app/src/server/objective/queries.ts
      modified_files:
        - schema/surreal-schema.surql
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-crud.test.ts

  - id: 01-02
    name: 'Behavior telemetry collection + scorer'
    description: >
      Create behavior table migration with exhibits relation and learning_evidence OUT extension.
      Implement behavior CRUD queries, pure scorer functions for metric normalization, and exhibits edge writer.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: []
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-03 #1: Behavior record created with metric_type, score (0-1), source_telemetry; exhibits edge links identity to behavior'
      - 'US-OB-03 #2: Multiple metric types per agent (tdd_adherence + security_first) each produce separate records'
      - 'US-OB-03 #4: New session creates new behavior record (append-only, no UPDATE)'
      - 'US-OB-03 #5: Agent with no sessions returns empty behavior array'
      - 'US-OB-03 #6: Score boundary values 0.0 and 1.0 accepted by ASSERT constraint'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Migration 0033 wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION'
      - 'scorer.ts is pure -- zero IO imports, maps telemetry to 0-1 float'
      - 'source_telemetry uses TYPE object FLEXIBLE for metric-specific shapes'
      - 'Behavior records are append-only at application layer'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - schema/migrations/0033_behavior_table.surql
        - app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts
        - app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts
      modified_files:
        - schema/surreal-schema.surql
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-telemetry.test.ts

  - id: 01-03
    name: 'Objective + behavior HTTP routes'
    description: >
      Expose objective CRUD and behavior score/trend queries via REST endpoints.
      Register routes in start-server.ts. Create fulltext search index migration for objective title.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-01, 01-02]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-01 #3: GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/objectives returns workspace-scoped objective list'
      - 'US-OB-03 #3: Behavior trend visible across 5 sessions via GET endpoint'
      - 'US-OB-03 #8: Behavior records returned are workspace-scoped'
      - 'POST creates objective, GET retrieves by ID, PUT updates status'
      - 'Behavior scores queryable by identity and metric_type'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Migration 0034 for fulltext index uses existing entity_search analyzer with BM25(1.2, 0.75)'
      - 'Route modules compose queries + pure logic at boundary only'
      - 'Routes registered in start-server.ts following existing pattern'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - schema/migrations/0034_objective_fulltext.surql
        - app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts
        - app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-crud.test.ts
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-telemetry.test.ts

- id: '02'
  name: 'Alignment and Policy'
  steps:
  - id: 02-01
    name: 'Intent-objective alignment evaluator'
    description: >
      Pure cosine similarity function classifying alignment result (matched/ambiguous/none).
      Supports edge writer creates intent->supports->objective edges. Integrate into authorizer
      after policy gate pass.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: [01-01]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-02 #1: Supports edge created with alignment_score when intent cosine similarity >= 0.7 to an active objective'
      - 'US-OB-02 #3: Manual supports edge records alignment_method as "manual"'
      - 'US-OB-02 #6: Multiple intents can support the same objective via separate supports edges'
      - 'Alignment failure does not block intent authorization (warning mode fallback)'
      - 'Two-step KNN pattern used for HNSW+workspace filter'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'alignment.ts is pure -- receives embeddings, returns AlignmentResult'
      - 'Supports edges are immutable once created'
      - 'KNN query uses two-step pattern per SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug'
      - 'Alignment step added after policy gate in authorizer pipeline'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/intent-alignment.test.ts

  - id: 02-02
    name: 'Behavior-based policy enforcement'
    description: >
      Enrich IntentEvaluationContext with behavior_scores before policy rule evaluation.
      Query latest score per identity+metric_type. Existing predicate evaluator resolves
      dot-path fields (behavior_scores.tdd_adherence) without schema changes.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-02]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-04 #1: Intent vetoed when behavior score below policy threshold (e.g., tdd_adherence < 0.6)'
      - 'US-OB-04 #2: Intent proceeds when all behavior scores above respective thresholds'
      - 'US-OB-04 #3: Testing-mode policy logs veto reason without blocking authorization'
      - 'US-OB-04 #5: Agent with no behavior data passes policy (no score = no veto)'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'No policy schema changes -- existing RulePredicate with dot-path resolution handles behavior_scores.*'
      - 'Behavior score lookup indexed on workspace + metric_type, ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1'
      - 'Context enrichment happens before policy gate evaluation, not inside it'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/policy/types.ts
        - app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-policy.test.ts

- id: '03'
  name: 'Visibility and Auditing'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'Objective progress visibility'
    description: >
      Compute supporting intent count per objective via supports edges.
      Track success criteria current_value vs target_value. Detect expired objectives by target_date.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [02-01]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-05 #1: Objective progress shows correct count of supporting intents'
      - 'US-OB-05 #2: Success criteria display current_value and target_value per KPI'
      - 'US-OB-05 #3: Objective with zero supporting intents identifiable as unsupported'
      - 'US-OB-05 #4: Objective past target_date detected as expired'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Progress computed via graph traversal (count <-supports<-intent), not denormalized counter'
      - 'Expiration is query-time check, not background job'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/objective/queries.ts
        - app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-progress.test.ts

  - id: 03-02
    name: 'Coherence auditor scans'
    description: >
      Extend observer graph-scan with coherence detection: orphaned decisions (confirmed, no implementing
      task/commit after 14d), stale objectives (active, no supports edges after 14d). Create observations
      for disconnected patterns.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-01]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-06 #1: Orphaned decision (confirmed, 14d+ old, no implementing task) detected and observation created'
      - 'US-OB-06 #2: Stale objective (active, 14d+ old, no supports edges) detected and observation created'
      - 'US-OB-06 #3: Connected objective with supports edges not flagged as stale'
      - 'US-OB-06 #5: Recently created decision (< 14d) not flagged as orphan regardless of implementation status'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Coherence queries are pure SurrealQL with LIMIT 50 per entity type'
      - 'New scan types added as functions called from existing runGraphScan orchestrator'
      - 'Observations use existing observes relation (extended OUT includes objective)'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/coherence-auditor.test.ts

  - id: 03-03
    name: 'Behavior trend computation'
    description: >
      Pure trend analysis functions: detect consecutive below-threshold streaks, improvement
      after learning injection, flat-line patterns. Operates on behavior record arrays with no DB access.
    time_estimate: 2h
    dependencies: [01-02]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-07 #1: 3+ consecutive below-threshold scores detected as drift pattern'
      - 'US-OB-07 #2: Improving scores after learning injection detectable as positive trend'
      - 'US-OB-07 #4: Flat scores after learning detected as ineffective'
      - 'US-OB-07 #6: Single session score does not trigger drift detection (requires 3+ data points)'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'trends.ts is pure -- zero IO imports, receives score arrays, returns trend classification'
      - 'Threshold and streak length are parameters, not hardcoded'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-learning.test.ts

- id: '04'
  name: 'Learning Loop'
  steps:
  - id: 04-01
    name: 'Behavior learning bridge'
    description: >
      Feed behavior trend signals into existing learning proposal pipeline. Observer queries
      recent behavior records, runs trend analysis, proposes learnings with behavior evidence.
      Rate-limited to 5 proposals per agent per 7 days.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [03-03]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-07 #3: Learning proposal contains correct observer metadata (source_agent, metric_type, evidence links)'
      - 'US-OB-07 #5: Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals per agent identity per 7 days'
      - 'Learning proposals use existing suggestLearning() with collision detection'
      - 'Behavior records linked as learning_evidence (via extended relation OUT)'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Uses existing learning API and collision detection -- no new learning creation path'
      - 'Behavior trend input alongside observation clusters in learning-diagnosis pipeline'
      - 'Fire-and-forget via inflight tracker -- failure does not block observer scan'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts
        - app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-learning.test.ts

  - id: 04-02
    name: 'Graph UI visualization for objective + behavior nodes'
    description: >
      Extend existing graph visualization components with objective and behavior node rendering,
      supports/exhibits/has_objective edge display. Leverage partially-implemented graph UI from
      commit f71e3c00.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-03]
    criteria:
      - 'Objective nodes render in graph view with title, status, priority'
      - 'Behavior nodes render with metric_type and score'
      - 'Supports edges visible between intent and objective nodes'
      - 'Exhibits edges visible between identity and behavior nodes'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Extend existing Reagraph graph components -- no new visualization library'
      - 'Node type colors/shapes follow existing graph UI conventions'
      - 'Graph data fetched from existing GET /api/graph/:workspaceId endpoint (extended)'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/client/
      test_files: []

implementation_scope:
  source_directories:
    - app/src/server/objective/
    - app/src/server/behavior/
    - app/src/client/
  test_directories:
    - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/
  excluded_patterns:
    - node_modules/**
    - dist/**
validation:
  status: pending
  reviewer: solution-architect-reviewer
  approved_at: pending

input.prompt:
<!-- DES-ENFORCEMENT : exempt -->

Review the roadmap at docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml for quality, completeness, and feasibility.

## Review Scope

Evaluate against these criteria:
1. **Completeness**: Do all steps cover the 7 user stories (US-OB-01 through US-OB-07) from the test scenarios?
2. **Acceptance criteria quality**: Are ACs behavioral and measurable? Do they reference specific test scenario IDs?
3. **Dependency correctness**: Do step dependencies match the walking skeleton dependency graph?
4. **Architectural constraints**: Are they consistent with CLAUDE.md conventions (functional paradigm, no null, SCHEMAFULL, RecordId, etc.)?
5. **Implementation scope accuracy**: Do file paths match the component-boundaries.md module decomposition?
6. **Step atomicity**: Is each step self-contained and testable independently?
7. **Risk assessment**: Any steps that seem too large, too vague, or missing key considerations?

## Context Files

Read these for review context:
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml (the artifact under review)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/walking-skeleton.md (implementation sequence)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/test-scenarios.md (all 47 test scenarios)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/component-boundaries.md (module decomposition)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/data-models.md (schema design)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/architecture-design.md (full architecture)
- CLAUDE.md (project conventions)

## Output Format

Provide:
1. APPROVE or REJECT (with specific blocking issues)
2. List of findings: CRITICAL (must fix) / HIGH (should fix) / LOW (nice to have)
3. If APPROVE: note any non-blocking improvements for implementation phase

TASK BOUNDARY: Review only. Do NOT modify any files. Return your verdict to the orchestrator.

content.text:
Now let me analyze the roadmap against the review criteria. I'll cross-reference the test scenarios, walking skeleton, and architecture design.

## Review Analysis

### 1. COMPLETENESS - Coverage of User Stories

Cross-checking roadmap steps against test scenarios (47 total, 7 stories):

| User Story | Roadmap Steps | Test Scenarios | Coverage |
|------------|---------------|-----------------|----------|
| US-OB-01 (Objectives) | 01-01, 01-03 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-02 (Alignment) | 02-01 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-03 (Behavior Telemetry) | 01-02, 01-03 | 8 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-04 (Policy Enforcement) | 02-02 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-05 (Progress Visibility) | 03-01 | 5 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-06 (Coherence Auditor) | 03-02 | 6 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-07 (Learning Loop) | 03-03, 04-01 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |

All 7 user stories are represented in the roadmap with corresponding test files.

### 2. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA QUALITY

Spot-checking criteria from roadmap against test scenarios:

**Step 01-01 (Objective CRUD)**: Maps to test scenarios US-OB-01 #1-5:
- "US-OB-01 #1: Objective created with title, priority, status, workspace scope and persisted to DB" ✓
- "US-OB-01 #4: Objective without target_date persists with field omitted (not null)" ✓ (behavioral, measurable)
- All criteria reference test scenario IDs (explicit traceability)

**Step 02-01 (Alignment)**: Maps to US-OB-02 scenarios:
- "US-OB-02 #1: Supports edge created with alignment_score when intent cosine similarity >= 0.7 to an active objective" ✓ (quantitative threshold)
- All criteria are behavioral and observable

**Step 02-02 (Behavior Policy)**: Maps to US-OB-04 scenarios:
- "US-OB-04 #1: Intent vetoed when behavior score below policy threshold (e.g., tdd_adherence < 0.6)" ✓ (measurable)
- All criteria are specific and testable

### 3. DEPENDENCY CORRECTNESS

Comparing roadmap dependencies (01-01 → 02-01 → 03-01) against walking-skeleton.md dependency graph:

**Roadmap Step Dependencies:**
- 01-01: [] ✓ (correct - objective CRUD is foundation)
- 01-02: [] ✓ (correct - behavior telemetry independent)
- 01-03: [01-01, 01-02] ✓ (correct - needs both CRUD modules)
- 02-01: [01-01] ✓ (correct - needs objectives for alignment)
- 02-02: [01-02] ✓ (correct - needs behavior for policy)
- 03-01: [02-01] ✓ (correct - needs supports edges for progress)
- 03-02: [01-01] ✓ (correct - objectives for stale detection)
- 03-03: [01-02] ✓ (correct - behavior records for trends)
- 04-01: [03-03] ✓ (correct - trends for learning bridge)
- 04-02: [01-03] ✓ (correct - needs HTTP routes for graph data)

**Walking skeleton declares:**
- Skeleton 1 → Skeleton 2, 5, 6
- Skeleton 3 → Skeleton 4, 7
- Roadmap matches this ordering exactly.

### 4. ARCHITECTURAL CONSTRAINTS

Checking against CLAUDE.md project conventions:

**Null handling**: Roadmap step 01-01 explicitly states: "All fields use option<T> for absence, never null" ✓

**Transaction wrapping**: Step 01-01 specifies "Migration 0032 wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION" ✓ (matches CLAUDE.md SurrealDB convention)

**RecordId usage**: Step 01-01 states "Query functions accept Surreal + RecordId params, return typed results" ✓

**HNSW indexing**: Step 01-01 specifies "HNSW index on embedding field (DIMENSION 1536, DIST COSINE)" ✓

**Pure functions**: Steps 01-02, 02-01, 03-03 explicitly mark pure modules with "pure scorer functions", "pure cosine similarity function", "pure trend analysis functions" ✓

**Functional paradigm**: Component boundaries state "functional paradigm -- composition pipelines, types-first, pure core / effect shell" ✓

**Fire-and-forget**: Step 04-01 states "Fire-and-forget via inflight tracker -- failure does not block observer scan" ✓ (matches CLAUDE.md fire-and-forget pattern)

All major architectural constraints are explicit in the roadmap.

### 5. IMPLEMENTATION SCOPE ACCURACY

Checking file paths against component-boundaries.md:

**New files in roadmap match component-boundaries exactly:**
- `app/src/server/objective/queries.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts` ✓

**Modified files match:**
- `schema/surreal-schema.surql` ✓
- `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` (route registration) ✓
- `app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts` (alignment step) ✓
- `app/src/server/policy/types.ts` (behavior context enrichment) ✓
- `app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts` (coherence + behavior telemetry) ✓
- `app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts` (behavior learning bridge) ✓
- `app/src/client/` (graph UI) ✓

All paths are consistent with component-boundaries.md module decomposition.

### 6. STEP ATOMICITY

Evaluating each step for self-containment:

| Step | Self-contained? | Rationale |
|------|-----------------|-----------|
| 01-01 | YES | Creates schema + CRUD functions. Tests via direct DB queries. No HTTP routes needed. |
| 01-02 | YES | Creates behavior schema + scorer. Independent of objective CRUD. |
| 01-03 | PARTIAL | Depends on 01-01 and 01-02 (declared). Routes are testable via acceptance tests. |
| 02-01 | YES | Pure alignment logic. Integrates into existing authorizer (one-touch). New tests isolated. |
| 02-02 | YES | Context enrichment only (one-touch to policy gate). No new tables. Scoped test. |
| 03-01 | PARTIAL | Extends queries from 01-01. New route logic. Depends on 02-01 (supports edges must exist). |
| 03-02 | YES | Pure graph queries. Extends existing observer (one-touch). Fire-and-forget safety. |
| 03-03 | YES | Pure trend analysis. No DB writes. Composable as input to 04-01. |
| 04-01 | YES | Feeds existing learning API. Uses existing collision detection. One-touch integration. |
| 04-02 | YES | UI-only. Extends existing Reagraph components. No DB changes. |

All steps are atomic or explicitly depend on predecessor steps. No circular dependencies.

### 7. RISK ASSESSMENT

**CRITICAL RISKS IDENTIFIED:**

1. **Embedding Generation Not Explicitly Scoped** (Step 01-01, 02-01)
   - Roadmap references "HNSW index on embedding field" but does not specify WHO generates embeddings
   - Data models show `embedding ON objective TYPE array<float>` but no step explicitly covers embedding pipeline integration
   - Walking skeleton marks 2 scenarios `Skip` due to "Embedding generation pipeline integration"
   - **Risk**: Unclear implementation path for embedding generation. Is this assumed to be existing extraction pipeline, or new work?

2. **Time Estimate 1h Shortfall on Step 01-03** (Indirect)
   - Step 01-03 depends on both 01-01 (4h) and 01-02 (4h) = 8h of prerequisites
   - Step 01-03 is estimated 3h but creates 2 route modules + fulltext migration
   - Routes need route registration in start-server.ts, which is a modification touch point
   - **Risk**: Underestimated scope for HTTP routing layer. 3h is tight for creating 2 route modules + tests + integration.

3. **Policy Behavior Enrichment Assumes Dot-Path Resolution Works** (Step 02-02)
   - Roadmap states "Existing predicate evaluator resolves dot-path fields (behavior_scores.tdd_adherence) without schema changes"
   - This relies on existing code behavior NOT being tested in the roadmap steps
   - No acceptance test for dot-path resolution on nested `behavior_scores` object
   - **Risk**: If dot-path resolution doesn't exist or is broken, step 02-02 will fail silently in predicate evaluation

4. **Observer Coherence Scan Timing (Step 03-02)**
   - Roadmap references "14d + old" thresholds for orphaned decisions/stale objectives
   - These are hardcoded in the description but not parameterized in criteria
   - Step 03-02 is 3h for adding coherence detection to existing observer, but coherence thresholds are never tested in acceptance scenarios
   - **Risk**: 14-day threshold is arbitrary. No configurable policy. Coherence detection acceptance test (coherence-auditor.test.ts) may not cover the threshold parameterization.

5. **Rate Limiting on Learning Proposals Not Implemented (Step 04-01)**
   - Test scenario US-OB-07 #5: "Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals per agent per 7 days"
   - Roadmap step 04-01 criteria states: "Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals per agent identity per 7 days"
   - But **no new files or code are listed** for rate limiting logic
   - Step 04-01 says "modified_files: []" -- only graph-scan.ts and learning-diagnosis.ts
   - **Risk**: Rate limiting logic is not scoped. Either it exists in learning API (undocumented) or must be added (missing from roadmap scope).

6. **Behavior Scoring Telemetry Shape Underspecified (Step 01-02)**
   - Data models show `source_telemetry TYPE object FLEXIBLE` with no DEFINE FIELD for sub-fields
   - Comments mention "metric-specific data shapes (TDD: files_changed, test_files_changed; Security: cve_advisories, addressed_count)" but these are NOT in schema
   - Scorer function (scorer.ts) must infer structure from unvalidated FLEXIBLE object
   - **Risk**: Scorer function tests will need to mock telemetry shapes that are not schema-validated. No contract enforcement between session data producer and scorer consumer.

7. **Graph UI Implementation Scope (Step 04-02) Vague**
   - Roadmap says: "Leverage partially-implemented graph UI from commit f71e3c00"
   - Does this mean the objective/behavior node rendering is ALREADY partially done?
   - Or is "partial" referring to the Reagraph integration itself?
   - Criteria don't specify which components need creation vs. extension
   - **Risk**: Unclear if 3h is sufficient or if code already exists. Test file list is empty (`test_files: []`), suggesting no new acceptance tests. But walking skeleton depends on graph data being fetchable.

**HIGH RISKS:**

8. **Behavior Policy Integration Test Coverage (Step 02-02)**
   - Acceptance test `behavior-policy.test.ts` tests veto scenarios
   - But the test only verifies behavior score lookup + intent status, not actual policy rule evaluation with nested dot-path
   - If predicate evaluator is broken or behavior_scores context is not passed correctly, tests may pass while real scenario fails
   - **Risk**: Acceptance test may not catch integration issues with existing policy gate code.

9. **KNN Bug Workaround Not Tested (Step 02-01, 03-01)**
   - Roadmap states: "Two-step KNN pattern used for HNSW+workspace filter" (per SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug)
   - Architectural design specifies this workaround
   - But there is NO acceptance test that specifically validates the two-step KNN query works (intent-alignment.test.ts scenario #7 is marked `Skip`)
   - **Risk**: The two-step KNN pattern might not be implemented, or might be implemented incorrectly, and the skipped @property test won't catch it.

### Summary of Findings

**CRITICAL BLOCKERS (Must fix before approval):**

- **D1**: Embedding generation integration is not explicitly scoped. No step declares responsibility for generating objective embeddings.
- **D2**: Rate limiting for learning proposals (US-OB-07 #5) is declared in test scenario but no implementation scope in step 04-01.
- **D3**: Behavior telemetry source shape is not schema-validated (FLEXIBLE object with no DEFINE FIELD sub-fields). Scorer function signature and test contract unclear.
- **D4**: Graph UI implementation scope (step 04-02) references "partially-implemented" code but doesn't specify what exists vs. what needs to be built.

**HIGH SEVERITY (Should fix):**

- **D5**: Step 01-03 time estimate (3h) may be underestimated given scope (2 route modules + 1 fulltext migration + route registration).
- **D6**: Dot-path resolution in policy predicate evaluator is assumed to work but not tested in acceptance scenarios. Risk of silent failure.
- **D7**: Coherence scan thresholds (14d) are hardcoded in description but not parameterized or tested.
- **D8**: KNN bug workaround (two-step pattern) is mentioned but not covered by non-skipped acceptance tests.
- **D9**: Behavior policy acceptance test doesn't validate integration with actual policy gate predicate evaluation (only score lookup).

---

## VERDICT

**REJECT** — 4 critical blockers prevent approval. The roadmap is comprehensive in structure but has gaps in implementation scoping that will cause rework or test failures during execution.

```yaml
review:
  verdict: REJECTED
  iteration: 1
  scope: Roadmap quality, completeness, and feasibility review
  evaluation_date: 2026-03-14
  
  defects:
    - id: D1
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Completeness / Implementation Scope
      location: "roadmap.yaml steps 01-01, 02-01 + data-models.md embedding field"
      description: |
        Embedding generation for objectives is not explicitly scoped to any roadmap step.
        Data models define `embedding ON objective TYPE array<float>` and step 01-01 
        references `HNSW index on embedding field` but no step declares responsibility for 
        generating embeddings during objective creation. Walking skeleton marks 2 scenarios 
        as `Skip` due to missing "Embedding generation pipeline integration", but this 
        dependency is never resolved in the roadmap. Who generates the embedding vector?
        Is it the extraction pipeline (assumed), or a new step?
      suggestion: |
        Add explicit embedding generation scope to step 01-01 or create a new step 01-01a 
        "Objective embedding generation" that integrates with existing extraction/embedding 
        pipeline. Document the contract: objectives created via extraction API receive 
        embeddings automatically; objectives created via direct HTTP API use placeholder 
        or trigger async embedding job.
      
    - id: D2
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Completeness / Acceptance Criteria Mapping
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 04-01, test-scenarios.md US-OB-07 #5"
      description: |
        Test scenario US-OB-07 #5 declares: "Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals 
        per agent identity per 7 days". Roadmap step 04-01 criteria mirrors this requirement. 
        However, step 04-01 implementation_scope shows `modified_files: []` with only 
        graph-scan.ts and learning-diagnosis.ts listed. No rate limiting logic appears 
        in any new file. Either the rate limiting exists in the existing learning API 
        (undocumented) or the requirement is not scoped. This is a blocker for acceptance 
        test behavior-learning.test.ts scenario #5.
      suggestion: |
        Either: (a) document that rate limiting already exists in the learning API and 
        reference the code path, or (b) add a new step 04-01a "Learning rate limiter for 
        behavior proposals" with specific scope (modify learning-diagnosis.ts or create 
        new learning-rate-limit.ts). Include acceptance test verification that 6th proposal 
        within 7 days is rejected.
      
    - id: D3
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Architectural Constraints / Schema Completeness
      location: "data-models.md behavior table, roadmap.yaml step 01-02"
      description: |
        Behavior table schema defines `source_telemetry ON behavior TYPE object FLEXIBLE` 
        with zero DEFINE FIELD sub-fields. Comments reference metric-specific shapes 
        ("TDD: files_changed, test_files_changed; Security: cve_advisories, addressed_count") 
        but these are not schema-validated. Scorer function (scorer.ts) must infer structure 
        from unvalidated FLEXIBLE object, violating CLAUDE.md "never rely on permissive 
        object inference" principle. This creates a contract mismatch between session 
        telemetry producer and scorer consumer.
      suggestion: |
        Either: (a) add DEFINE FIELD for known sub-fields (source_telemetry.files_changed, 
        source_telemetry.test_ratio, etc.) as TYPE option<...> FLEXIBLE, or (b) strongly 
        type `source_telemetry` as a union type in scorer.ts with runtime validation. 
        Update step 01-02 acceptance test to verify telemetry shape contracts per metric 
        type.
      
    - id: D4
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Completeness / Implementation Scope
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 04-02 (Graph UI visualization)"
      description: |
        Step 04-02 states "Leverage partially-implemented graph UI from commit f71e3c00" 
        but does not specify what is already implemented vs. what must be built. Criteria 
        list 4 observable outcomes (objective nodes render, behavior nodes render, supports 
        edges visible, exhibits edges visible) but implementation_scope shows no new_files 
        listed (only `modified_files: app/src/client/`). Test file list is empty, which 
        contradicts the walking skeleton dependency: graph data must be fetchable via 
        GET /api/graph endpoint. Unclear if this is a schema/query extension or a pure UI 
        component addition.
      suggestion: |
        Clarify step 04-02 scope: (a) List specific components to create/modify in 
        app/src/client/ (e.g., ObjectiveNode.tsx, BehaviorNode.tsx, supports edge renderer), 
        (b) document what code already exists in commit f71e3c00, (c) add acceptance test 
        or E2E test that verifies objective + behavior nodes are renderable by fetching 
        graph data from GET /api/graph endpoint.
  
    - id: D5
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Risk Assessment / Time Estimation
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 01-03 (time_estimate: 3h)"
      description: |
        Step 01-03 depends on both 01-01 (4h) and 01-02 (4h) of prior work and creates 
        2 new route modules (objective-route.ts, behavior-route.ts) plus 1 fulltext migration 
        (0034_objective_fulltext.surql) plus route registration in start-server.ts. 
        Estimated 3h is tight. Route integration typically requires: handler implementation, 
        parameter validation, error handling, tests, and potential iteration with test 
        failures. Each route module is ~100-150 LoC. Starting 8h into the task with reduced 
        context and fresh focus on routing logic is risk.
      suggestion: |
        Increase 01-03 estimate to 4-5h to account for route integration complexity 
        and test iteration. Or decompose into 01-03a (objective routes, 2h) and 01-03b 
        (behavior routes + fulltext, 2h) for incremental commit points.
      
    - id: D6
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Architectural Constraints / Integration Risk
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 02-02 (policy context enrichment)"
      description: |
        Step 02-02 criteria state "No policy schema changes -- existing RulePredicate with 
        dot-path resolution handles behavior_scores.*". This assumes the predicate evaluator 
        already supports nested dot-path resolution (e.g., "behavior_scores.tdd_adherence"). 
        However, the acceptance test (behavior-policy.test.ts) only verifies behavior score 
        lookup and intent veto, not the actual predicate evaluation with nested field 
        resolution. If the predicate evaluator doesn't support dot-paths or breaks with 
        nested objects, the integration will fail silently (intent proceeding when it should 
        veto, or vice versa).
      suggestion: |
        Add an explicit acceptance test scenario to behavior-policy.test.ts that validates 
        predicate evaluation with dot-path fields: create a policy rule with 
        `field: "behavior_scores.tdd_adherence", operator: "lt", value: 0.6`, submit an 
        intent with a behavior score at 0.5, and verify the veto occurs. Document the 
        predicate evaluator's dot-path implementation or add it if missing.
      
    - id: D7
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Architectural Constraints / Parameterization
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 03-02 (Coherence auditor), data-models.md key query patterns"
      description: |
        Step 03-02 criteria reference a hardcoded "14d" threshold for orphaned decisions 
        and stale objectives. The description states `created_at < $threshold_date` but 
        the 14-day value is never parameterized as a configurable constant or policy setting. 
        This creates technical debt: if the threshold needs to change, it requires a code 
        change and recompile, not a configuration update. No acceptance test (coherence-auditor.test.ts) 
        validates the threshold parameterization.
      suggestion: |
        Extract the 14-day threshold as a constant in graph-scan.ts (e.g., 
        `const ORPHAN_AGE_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 14`) with a comment explaining the policy. 
        Or, store the threshold in SurrealDB as a workspace configuration parameter. Add 
        an acceptance test scenario that validates the threshold: create a decision at 
        13d ago and verify it is NOT flagged; create another at 15d ago and verify it IS 
        flagged.
      
    - id: D8
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Completeness / Walking Skeleton Coverage
      location: "test-scenarios.md US-OB-02 #7 (@property test), intent-alignment.test.ts"
      description: |
        Test scenario US-OB-02 #7 is marked @property and tagged `Skip`. It validates that 
        alignment evaluation completes within 200ms. The roadmap step 02-01 states "Must 
        complete within 200ms" as an architectural constraint. However, the performance 
        property test is not in the active acceptance test suite. If the two-step KNN 
        workaround or alignment logic is implemented inefficiently, the constraint will be 
        violated and not caught until later. The walking skeleton does not require this 
        @property test to pass, but it should be unblocked early.
      suggestion: |
        Clarify whether the 200ms constraint is a hard requirement or best-effort. If hard, 
        add a non-skipped scenario to intent-alignment.test.ts that performs a basic timing 
        check (e.g., measure 10 alignments and assert median < 200ms). Or, document when 
        the @property test will be enabled (during performance tuning phase).
      
    - id: D9
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Completeness / Acceptance Test Coverage
      location: "test-scenarios.md US-OB-04 (behavior-policy), behavior-policy.test.ts"
      description: |
        Test scenario US-OB-04 #1 declares "Intent vetoed when behavior score below policy 
        threshold". Acceptance test behavior-policy.test.ts will verify this by: (1) creating 
        a behavior record with score < 0.6, (2) submitting an intent, (3) checking intent 
        status. However, this does NOT validate that the policy rule predicate evaluation 
        actually resolved the nested behavior_scores field correctly. If the context enrichment 
        fails or the predicate evaluator doesn't unpack dot-paths, the test might pass (intent 
        status changes) but for the wrong reason (e.g., a different policy rule fired).
      suggestion: |
        Add an explicit acceptance test that validates the predicate evaluation path: 
        create a policy with a behavior_scores.* rule, enable verbose logging or 
        observability, and verify the veto reason explicitly includes the behavior metric 
        name. Or, mock the policy gate to intercept IntentEvaluationContext and assert 
        behavior_scores was populated before predicate evaluation.

    generation is referenced but not scoped, (2) rate limiting for learning proposals is
```

<!-- DES-ENFORCEMENT : exempt -->

Review the roadmap at docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml for quality, completeness, and feasibility.

## Review Scope

Evaluate against these criteria:
1. **Completeness**: Do all steps cover the 7 user stories (US-OB-01 through US-OB-07) from the test scenarios?
2. **Acceptance criteria quality**: Are ACs behavioral and measurable? Do they reference specific test scenario IDs?
3. **Dependency correctness**: Do step dependencies match the walking skeleton dependency graph?
4. **Architectural constraints**: Are they consistent with CLAUDE.md conventions (functional paradigm, no null, SCHEMAFULL, RecordId, etc.)?
5. **Implementation scope accuracy**: Do file paths match the component-boundaries.md module decomposition?
6. **Step atomicity**: Is each step self-contained and testable independently?
7. **Risk assessment**: Any steps that seem too large, too vague, or missing key considerations?

## Context Files

Read these for review context:
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml (the artifact under review)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/walking-skeleton.md (implementation sequence)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/test-scenarios.md (all 47 test scenarios)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/component-boundaries.md (module decomposition)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/data-models.md (schema design)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/architecture-design.md (full architecture)
- CLAUDE.md (project conventions)

## Output Format

Provide:
1. APPROVE or REJECT (with specific blocking issues)
2. List of findings: CRITICAL (must fix) / HIGH (should fix) / LOW (nice to have)
3. If APPROVE: note any non-blocking improvements for implementation phase

TASK BOUNDARY: Review only. Do NOT modify any files. Return your verdict to the orchestrator.

content.text:
Now let me analyze the roadmap against the review criteria. I'll cross-reference the test scenarios, walking skeleton, and architecture design.

## Review Analysis

### 1. COMPLETENESS - Coverage of User Stories

Cross-checking roadmap steps against test scenarios (47 total, 7 stories):

| User Story | Roadmap Steps | Test Scenarios | Coverage |
|------------|---------------|-----------------|----------|
| US-OB-01 (Objectives) | 01-01, 01-03 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-02 (Alignment) | 02-01 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-03 (Behavior Telemetry) | 01-02, 01-03 | 8 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-04 (Policy Enforcement) | 02-02 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-05 (Progress Visibility) | 03-01 | 5 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-06 (Coherence Auditor) | 03-02 | 6 scenarios | COMPLETE |
| US-OB-07 (Learning Loop) | 03-03, 04-01 | 7 scenarios | COMPLETE |

All 7 user stories are represented in the roadmap with corresponding test files.

### 2. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA QUALITY

Spot-checking criteria from roadmap against test scenarios:

**Step 01-01 (Objective CRUD)**: Maps to test scenarios US-OB-01 #1-5:
- "US-OB-01 #1: Objective created with title, priority, status, workspace scope and persisted to DB" ✓
- "US-OB-01 #4: Objective without target_date persists with field omitted (not null)" ✓ (behavioral, measurable)
- All criteria reference test scenario IDs (explicit traceability)

**Step 02-01 (Alignment)**: Maps to US-OB-02 scenarios:
- "US-OB-02 #1: Supports edge created with alignment_score when intent cosine similarity >= 0.7 to an active objective" ✓ (quantitative threshold)
- All criteria are behavioral and observable

**Step 02-02 (Behavior Policy)**: Maps to US-OB-04 scenarios:
- "US-OB-04 #1: Intent vetoed when behavior score below policy threshold (e.g., tdd_adherence < 0.6)" ✓ (measurable)
- All criteria are specific and testable

### 3. DEPENDENCY CORRECTNESS

Comparing roadmap dependencies (01-01 → 02-01 → 03-01) against walking-skeleton.md dependency graph:

**Roadmap Step Dependencies:**
- 01-01: [] ✓ (correct - objective CRUD is foundation)
- 01-02: [] ✓ (correct - behavior telemetry independent)
- 01-03: [01-01, 01-02] ✓ (correct - needs both CRUD modules)
- 02-01: [01-01] ✓ (correct - needs objectives for alignment)
- 02-02: [01-02] ✓ (correct - needs behavior for policy)
- 03-01: [02-01] ✓ (correct - needs supports edges for progress)
- 03-02: [01-01] ✓ (correct - objectives for stale detection)
- 03-03: [01-02] ✓ (correct - behavior records for trends)
- 04-01: [03-03] ✓ (correct - trends for learning bridge)
- 04-02: [01-03] ✓ (correct - needs HTTP routes for graph data)

**Walking skeleton declares:**
- Skeleton 1 → Skeleton 2, 5, 6
- Skeleton 3 → Skeleton 4, 7
- Roadmap matches this ordering exactly.

### 4. ARCHITECTURAL CONSTRAINTS

Checking against CLAUDE.md project conventions:

**Null handling**: Roadmap step 01-01 explicitly states: "All fields use option<T> for absence, never null" ✓

**Transaction wrapping**: Step 01-01 specifies "Migration 0032 wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION" ✓ (matches CLAUDE.md SurrealDB convention)

**RecordId usage**: Step 01-01 states "Query functions accept Surreal + RecordId params, return typed results" ✓

**HNSW indexing**: Step 01-01 specifies "HNSW index on embedding field (DIMENSION 1536, DIST COSINE)" ✓

**Pure functions**: Steps 01-02, 02-01, 03-03 explicitly mark pure modules with "pure scorer functions", "pure cosine similarity function", "pure trend analysis functions" ✓

**Functional paradigm**: Component boundaries state "functional paradigm -- composition pipelines, types-first, pure core / effect shell" ✓

**Fire-and-forget**: Step 04-01 states "Fire-and-forget via inflight tracker -- failure does not block observer scan" ✓ (matches CLAUDE.md fire-and-forget pattern)

All major architectural constraints are explicit in the roadmap.

### 5. IMPLEMENTATION SCOPE ACCURACY

Checking file paths against component-boundaries.md:

**New files in roadmap match component-boundaries exactly:**
- `app/src/server/objective/queries.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts` ✓
- `app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts` ✓

**Modified files match:**
- `schema/surreal-schema.surql` ✓
- `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` (route registration) ✓
- `app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts` (alignment step) ✓
- `app/src/server/policy/types.ts` (behavior context enrichment) ✓
- `app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts` (coherence + behavior telemetry) ✓
- `app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts` (behavior learning bridge) ✓
- `app/src/client/` (graph UI) ✓

All paths are consistent with component-boundaries.md module decomposition.

### 6. STEP ATOMICITY

Evaluating each step for self-containment:

| Step | Self-contained? | Rationale |
|------|-----------------|-----------|
| 01-01 | YES | Creates schema + CRUD functions. Tests via direct DB queries. No HTTP routes needed. |
| 01-02 | YES | Creates behavior schema + scorer. Independent of objective CRUD. |
| 01-03 | PARTIAL | Depends on 01-01 and 01-02 (declared). Routes are testable via acceptance tests. |
| 02-01 | YES | Pure alignment logic. Integrates into existing authorizer (one-touch). New tests isolated. |
| 02-02 | YES | Context enrichment only (one-touch to policy gate). No new tables. Scoped test. |
| 03-01 | PARTIAL | Extends queries from 01-01. New route logic. Depends on 02-01 (supports edges must exist). |
| 03-02 | YES | Pure graph queries. Extends existing observer (one-touch). Fire-and-forget safety. |
| 03-03 | YES | Pure trend analysis. No DB writes. Composable as input to 04-01. |
| 04-01 | YES | Feeds existing learning API. Uses existing collision detection. One-touch integration. |
| 04-02 | YES | UI-only. Extends existing Reagraph components. No DB changes. |

All steps are atomic or explicitly depend on predecessor steps. No circular dependencies.

### 7. RISK ASSESSMENT

**CRITICAL RISKS IDENTIFIED:**

1. **Embedding Generation Not Explicitly Scoped** (Step 01-01, 02-01)
   - Roadmap references "HNSW index on embedding field" but does not specify WHO generates embeddings
   - Data models show `embedding ON objective TYPE array<float>` but no step explicitly covers embedding pipeline integration
   - Walking skeleton marks 2 scenarios `Skip` due to "Embedding generation pipeline integration"
   - **Risk**: Unclear implementation path for embedding generation. Is this assumed to be existing extraction pipeline, or new work?

2. **Time Estimate 1h Shortfall on Step 01-03** (Indirect)
   - Step 01-03 depends on both 01-01 (4h) and 01-02 (4h) = 8h of prerequisites
   - Step 01-03 is estimated 3h but creates 2 route modules + fulltext migration
   - Routes need route registration in start-server.ts, which is a modification touch point
   - **Risk**: Underestimated scope for HTTP routing layer. 3h is tight for creating 2 route modules + tests + integration.

3. **Policy Behavior Enrichment Assumes Dot-Path Resolution Works** (Step 02-02)
   - Roadmap states "Existing predicate evaluator resolves dot-path fields (behavior_scores.tdd_adherence) without schema changes"
   - This relies on existing code behavior NOT being tested in the roadmap steps
   - No acceptance test for dot-path resolution on nested `behavior_scores` object
   - **Risk**: If dot-path resolution doesn't exist or is broken, step 02-02 will fail silently in predicate evaluation

4. **Observer Coherence Scan Timing (Step 03-02)**
   - Roadmap references "14d + old" thresholds for orphaned decisions/stale objectives
   - These are hardcoded in the description but not parameterized in criteria
   - Step 03-02 is 3h for adding coherence detection to existing observer, but coherence thresholds are never tested in acceptance scenarios
   - **Risk**: 14-day threshold is arbitrary. No configurable policy. Coherence detection acceptance test (coherence-auditor.test.ts) may not cover the threshold parameterization.

5. **Rate Limiting on Learning Proposals Not Implemented (Step 04-01)**
   - Test scenario US-OB-07 #5: "Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals per agent per 7 days"
   - Roadmap step 04-01 criteria states: "Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals per agent identity per 7 days"
   - But **no new files or code are listed** for rate limiting logic
   - Step 04-01 says "modified_files: []" -- only graph-scan.ts and learning-diagnosis.ts
   - **Risk**: Rate limiting logic is not scoped. Either it exists in learning API (undocumented) or must be added (missing from roadmap scope).

6. **Behavior Scoring Telemetry Shape Underspecified (Step 01-02)**
   - Data models show `source_telemetry TYPE object FLEXIBLE` with no DEFINE FIELD for sub-fields
   - Comments mention "metric-specific data shapes (TDD: files_changed, test_files_changed; Security: cve_advisories, addressed_count)" but these are NOT in schema
   - Scorer function (scorer.ts) must infer structure from unvalidated FLEXIBLE object
   - **Risk**: Scorer function tests will need to mock telemetry shapes that are not schema-validated. No contract enforcement between session data producer and scorer consumer.

7. **Graph UI Implementation Scope (Step 04-02) Vague**
   - Roadmap says: "Leverage partially-implemented graph UI from commit f71e3c00"
   - Does this mean the objective/behavior node rendering is ALREADY partially done?
   - Or is "partial" referring to the Reagraph integration itself?
   - Criteria don't specify which components need creation vs. extension
   - **Risk**: Unclear if 3h is sufficient or if code already exists. Test file list is empty (`test_files: []`), suggesting no new acceptance tests. But walking skeleton depends on graph data being fetchable.

**HIGH RISKS:**

8. **Behavior Policy Integration Test Coverage (Step 02-02)**
   - Acceptance test `behavior-policy.test.ts` tests veto scenarios
   - But the test only verifies behavior score lookup + intent status, not actual policy rule evaluation with nested dot-path
   - If predicate evaluator is broken or behavior_scores context is not passed correctly, tests may pass while real scenario fails
   - **Risk**: Acceptance test may not catch integration issues with existing policy gate code.

9. **KNN Bug Workaround Not Tested (Step 02-01, 03-01)**
   - Roadmap states: "Two-step KNN pattern used for HNSW+workspace filter" (per SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug)
   - Architectural design specifies this workaround
   - But there is NO acceptance test that specifically validates the two-step KNN query works (intent-alignment.test.ts scenario #7 is marked `Skip`)
   - **Risk**: The two-step KNN pattern might not be implemented, or might be implemented incorrectly, and the skipped @property test won't catch it.

### Summary of Findings

**CRITICAL BLOCKERS (Must fix before approval):**

- **D1**: Embedding generation integration is not explicitly scoped. No step declares responsibility for generating objective embeddings.
- **D2**: Rate limiting for learning proposals (US-OB-07 #5) is declared in test scenario but no implementation scope in step 04-01.
- **D3**: Behavior telemetry source shape is not schema-validated (FLEXIBLE object with no DEFINE FIELD sub-fields). Scorer function signature and test contract unclear.
- **D4**: Graph UI implementation scope (step 04-02) references "partially-implemented" code but doesn't specify what exists vs. what needs to be built.

**HIGH SEVERITY (Should fix):**

- **D5**: Step 01-03 time estimate (3h) may be underestimated given scope (2 route modules + 1 fulltext migration + route registration).
- **D6**: Dot-path resolution in policy predicate evaluator is assumed to work but not tested in acceptance scenarios. Risk of silent failure.
- **D7**: Coherence scan thresholds (14d) are hardcoded in description but not parameterized or tested.
- **D8**: KNN bug workaround (two-step pattern) is mentioned but not covered by non-skipped acceptance tests.
- **D9**: Behavior policy acceptance test doesn't validate integration with actual policy gate predicate evaluation (only score lookup).

---

## VERDICT

**REJECT** — 4 critical blockers prevent approval. The roadmap is comprehensive in structure but has gaps in implementation scoping that will cause rework or test failures during execution.

  evaluation_date: 2026-03-14
  
  defects:
    - id: D1
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Completeness / Implementation Scope
      location: "roadmap.yaml steps 01-01, 02-01 + data-models.md embedding field"
      description: |
        Embedding generation for objectives is not explicitly scoped to any roadmap step.
        Data models define `embedding ON objective TYPE array<float>` and step 01-01 
        references `HNSW index on embedding field` but no step declares responsibility for 
        generating embeddings during objective creation. Walking skeleton marks 2 scenarios 
        as `Skip` due to missing "Embedding generation pipeline integration", but this 
        dependency is never resolved in the roadmap. Who generates the embedding vector?
        Is it the extraction pipeline (assumed), or a new step?
      suggestion: |
        Add explicit embedding generation scope to step 01-01 or create a new step 01-01a 
        "Objective embedding generation" that integrates with existing extraction/embedding 
        pipeline. Document the contract: objectives created via extraction API receive 
        embeddings automatically; objectives created via direct HTTP API use placeholder 
        or trigger async embedding job.
      
    - id: D2
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Completeness / Acceptance Criteria Mapping
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 04-01, test-scenarios.md US-OB-07 #5"
      description: |
        Test scenario US-OB-07 #5 declares: "Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals 
        per agent identity per 7 days". Roadmap step 04-01 criteria mirrors this requirement. 
        However, step 04-01 implementation_scope shows `modified_files: []` with only 
        graph-scan.ts and learning-diagnosis.ts listed. No rate limiting logic appears 
        in any new file. Either the rate limiting exists in the existing learning API 
        (undocumented) or the requirement is not scoped. This is a blocker for acceptance 
        test behavior-learning.test.ts scenario #5.
      suggestion: |
        Either: (a) document that rate limiting already exists in the learning API and 
        reference the code path, or (b) add a new step 04-01a "Learning rate limiter for 
        behavior proposals" with specific scope (modify learning-diagnosis.ts or create 
        new learning-rate-limit.ts). Include acceptance test verification that 6th proposal 
        within 7 days is rejected.
      
    - id: D3
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Architectural Constraints / Schema Completeness
      location: "data-models.md behavior table, roadmap.yaml step 01-02"
      description: |
        Behavior table schema defines `source_telemetry ON behavior TYPE object FLEXIBLE` 
        with zero DEFINE FIELD sub-fields. Comments reference metric-specific shapes 
        ("TDD: files_changed, test_files_changed; Security: cve_advisories, addressed_count") 
        but these are not schema-validated. Scorer function (scorer.ts) must infer structure 
        from unvalidated FLEXIBLE object, violating CLAUDE.md "never rely on permissive 
        object inference" principle. This creates a contract mismatch between session 
        telemetry producer and scorer consumer.
      suggestion: |
        Either: (a) add DEFINE FIELD for known sub-fields (source_telemetry.files_changed, 
        source_telemetry.test_ratio, etc.) as TYPE option<...> FLEXIBLE, or (b) strongly 
        type `source_telemetry` as a union type in scorer.ts with runtime validation. 
        Update step 01-02 acceptance test to verify telemetry shape contracts per metric 
        type.
      
    - id: D4
      severity: CRITICAL
      dimension: Completeness / Implementation Scope
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 04-02 (Graph UI visualization)"
      description: |
        Step 04-02 states "Leverage partially-implemented graph UI from commit f71e3c00" 
        but does not specify what is already implemented vs. what must be built. Criteria 
        list 4 observable outcomes (objective nodes render, behavior nodes render, supports 
        edges visible, exhibits edges visible) but implementation_scope shows no new_files 
        listed (only `modified_files: app/src/client/`). Test file list is empty, which 
        contradicts the walking skeleton dependency: graph data must be fetchable via 
        GET /api/graph endpoint. Unclear if this is a schema/query extension or a pure UI 
        component addition.
      suggestion: |
        Clarify step 04-02 scope: (a) List specific components to create/modify in 
        app/src/client/ (e.g., ObjectiveNode.tsx, BehaviorNode.tsx, supports edge renderer), 
        (b) document what code already exists in commit f71e3c00, (c) add acceptance test 
        or E2E test that verifies objective + behavior nodes are renderable by fetching 
        graph data from GET /api/graph endpoint.
  
    - id: D5
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Risk Assessment / Time Estimation
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 01-03 (time_estimate: 3h)"
      description: |
        Step 01-03 depends on both 01-01 (4h) and 01-02 (4h) of prior work and creates 
        2 new route modules (objective-route.ts, behavior-route.ts) plus 1 fulltext migration 
        (0034_objective_fulltext.surql) plus route registration in start-server.ts. 
        Estimated 3h is tight. Route integration typically requires: handler implementation, 
        parameter validation, error handling, tests, and potential iteration with test 
        failures. Each route module is ~100-150 LoC. Starting 8h into the task with reduced 
        context and fresh focus on routing logic is risk.
      suggestion: |
        Increase 01-03 estimate to 4-5h to account for route integration complexity 
        and test iteration. Or decompose into 01-03a (objective routes, 2h) and 01-03b 
        (behavior routes + fulltext, 2h) for incremental commit points.
      
    - id: D6
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Architectural Constraints / Integration Risk
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 02-02 (policy context enrichment)"
      description: |
        Step 02-02 criteria state "No policy schema changes -- existing RulePredicate with 
        dot-path resolution handles behavior_scores.*". This assumes the predicate evaluator 
        already supports nested dot-path resolution (e.g., "behavior_scores.tdd_adherence"). 
        However, the acceptance test (behavior-policy.test.ts) only verifies behavior score 
        lookup and intent veto, not the actual predicate evaluation with nested field 
        resolution. If the predicate evaluator doesn't support dot-paths or breaks with 
        nested objects, the integration will fail silently (intent proceeding when it should 
        veto, or vice versa).
      suggestion: |
        Add an explicit acceptance test scenario to behavior-policy.test.ts that validates 
        predicate evaluation with dot-path fields: create a policy rule with 
        `field: "behavior_scores.tdd_adherence", operator: "lt", value: 0.6`, submit an 
        intent with a behavior score at 0.5, and verify the veto occurs. Document the 
        predicate evaluator's dot-path implementation or add it if missing.
      
    - id: D7
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Architectural Constraints / Parameterization
      location: "roadmap.yaml step 03-02 (Coherence auditor), data-models.md key query patterns"
      description: |
        Step 03-02 criteria reference a hardcoded "14d" threshold for orphaned decisions 
        and stale objectives. The description states `created_at < $threshold_date` but 
        the 14-day value is never parameterized as a configurable constant or policy setting. 
        This creates technical debt: if the threshold needs to change, it requires a code 
        change and recompile, not a configuration update. No acceptance test (coherence-auditor.test.ts) 
        validates the threshold parameterization.
      suggestion: |
        Extract the 14-day threshold as a constant in graph-scan.ts (e.g., 
        `const ORPHAN_AGE_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 14`) with a comment explaining the policy. 
        Or, store the threshold in SurrealDB as a workspace configuration parameter. Add 
        an acceptance test scenario that validates the threshold: create a decision at 
        13d ago and verify it is NOT flagged; create another at 15d ago and verify it IS 
        flagged.
      
    - id: D8
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Completeness / Walking Skeleton Coverage
      location: "test-scenarios.md US-OB-02 #7 (@property test), intent-alignment.test.ts"
      description: |
        Test scenario US-OB-02 #7 is marked @property and tagged `Skip`. It validates that 
        alignment evaluation completes within 200ms. The roadmap step 02-01 states "Must 
        complete within 200ms" as an architectural constraint. However, the performance 
        property test is not in the active acceptance test suite. If the two-step KNN 
        workaround or alignment logic is implemented inefficiently, the constraint will be 
        violated and not caught until later. The walking skeleton does not require this 
        @property test to pass, but it should be unblocked early.
      suggestion: |
        Clarify whether the 200ms constraint is a hard requirement or best-effort. If hard, 
        add a non-skipped scenario to intent-alignment.test.ts that performs a basic timing 
        check (e.g., measure 10 alignments and assert median < 200ms). Or, document when 
        the @property test will be enabled (during performance tuning phase).
      
    - id: D9
      severity: HIGH
      dimension: Completeness / Acceptance Test Coverage
      location: "test-scenarios.md US-OB-04 (behavior-policy), behavior-policy.test.ts"
      description: |
        Test scenario US-OB-04 #1 declares "Intent vetoed when behavior score below policy 
        threshold". Acceptance test behavior-policy.test.ts will verify this by: (1) creating 
        a behavior record with score < 0.6, (2) submitting an intent, (3) checking intent 
        status. However, this does NOT validate that the policy rule predicate evaluation 
        actually resolved the nested behavior_scores field correctly. If the context enrichment 
        fails or the predicate evaluator doesn't unpack dot-paths, the test might pass (intent 
        status changes) but for the wrong reason (e.g., a different policy rule fired).
      suggestion: |
        Add an explicit acceptance test that validates the predicate evaluation path: 
        create a policy with a behavior_scores.* rule, enable verbose logging or 
        observability, and verify the veto reason explicitly includes the behavior metric 
        name. Or, mock the policy gate to intercept IntentEvaluationContext and assert 
        behavior_scores was populated before predicate evaluation.

<!-- DES-ENFORCEMENT : exempt -->

Fill in the roadmap skeleton at docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml.
Replace every TODO with real content. Do NOT change the YAML structure (phases, steps, keys). Fill in: names, descriptions, acceptance criteria, time estimates, dependencies, and implementation_scope paths.

Goal: Implement objective and behavior node types with alignment evaluation, behavior telemetry, policy enforcement, coherence auditing, and behavior learning loop.

## CRITICAL CONTEXT

Read these files for full context before filling the roadmap:
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/architecture-design.md (full architecture)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/data-models.md (SurrealDB schema)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/component-boundaries.md (module decomposition)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/walking-skeleton.md (implementation sequence + dependency graph)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/test-scenarios.md (all 47 acceptance test scenarios)
- CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md (project conventions)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql (existing schema — read before writing anything that references tables/fields)

## STEP MAPPING FROM WALKING SKELETON

The distill artifacts define 7 skeletons in a dependency graph. Map these into roadmap steps:

### Phase 1: Graph Layer Foundation (schema + CRUD)
- Step 01-01: Schema migrations (0032_objective_table.surql, 0033_behavior_table.surql, 0034_objective_fulltext.surql) + objective CRUD queries (app/src/server/objective/queries.ts)
- Step 01-02: Behavior telemetry collection — behavior CRUD queries + scorer (pure) + exhibits edge (app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts, app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts)
- Step 01-03: Objective and behavior HTTP routes (app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts, app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts) + route registration in start-server.ts

### Phase 2: Alignment and Policy
- Step 02-01: Intent-objective alignment evaluator — pure cosine similarity + supports edge writer (app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts, extends intent/authorizer.ts)
- Step 02-02: Behavior-based policy enforcement — enrich IntentEvaluationContext with behavior_scores, policy gate queries latest scores (extends policy/types.ts, policy/policy-gate.ts)

### Phase 3: Visibility and Auditing
- Step 03-01: Objective progress visibility — supporting intent count, success criteria tracking (extends objective/queries.ts, objective-route.ts)
- Step 03-02: Coherence auditor — orphaned decisions + stale objectives detection (extends observer/graph-scan.ts)
- Step 03-03: Behavior trend computation (app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts) — pure trend analysis functions

### Phase 4: Learning Loop
- Step 04-01: Behavior learning bridge — feeds behavior trends into existing learning proposal pipeline (extends observer/learning-diagnosis.ts)
- Step 04-02: Graph UI visualization for objective and behavior nodes (app/src/client/ — extend existing graph components, already partially done per git history)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA GUIDANCE

For each step, acceptance criteria MUST:
- Reference specific test scenarios from test-scenarios.md by ID (e.g., "US-OB-01 scenario 1: Leader creates objective")
- Be behavioral and measurable (Given/When/Then or assertion-style)
- Cover the walking skeleton scenario + key focused scenarios for that step

## KEY CONVENTIONS FROM CLAUDE.md

- Development paradigm: functional (pure core / effect shell)
- SurrealDB: SCHEMAFULL tables, no null (use option of T), RecordId objects everywhere
- Migrations: zero-padded prefix (0032, 0033, 0034), wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION (except DEFINE ANALYZER)
- No backwards compatibility / no data migration scripts
- Step IDs: NN-NN format (01-01, 01-02, etc.)

## FILES_TO_MODIFY

List actual file paths in implementation_scope. New files:
- schema/migrations/0032_objective_table.surql
- schema/migrations/0033_behavior_table.surql
- schema/migrations/0034_objective_fulltext.surql
- app/src/server/objective/queries.ts
- app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts
- app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts
- app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts

Extended files:
- app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts (route registration)
- app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts (alignment step)
- app/src/server/policy/types.ts (behavior_scores in context)
- app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts (coherence + telemetry scans)
- app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts (behavior trend signal)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql (updated base schema)

Test files:
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-crud.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-telemetry.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/intent-alignment.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-policy.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-progress.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/coherence-auditor.test.ts
- tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-learning.test.ts

TASK BOUNDARY: Fill the roadmap skeleton only. Do NOT implement any code. Return control to orchestrator after the roadmap is complete.

1→---
     2→name: roadmap-design
     3→description: Roadmap concision rules, step decomposition efficiency, AC abstraction guidelines, and step-to-scenario mapping. Load when creating implementation roadmaps.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Design
     7→
     8→## Canonical Format
     9→
    10→Use ONLY compact nested format. See `nWave/templates/roadmap-schema.yaml` for fields/validation. Structure: `roadmap` metadata, `phases` with nested `steps`, `implementation_scope`, `validation`. Phase IDs: two digits (`"01"`). Step IDs: `"NN-MM"`.
    11→
    12→## Concision Requirements
    13→
    14→Token efficiency: crafter reads roadmap ~35 times (7 phases x 5 steps). 5000-token x 35 = 175k tokens. 1000-token x 35 = 35k (80% savings).
    15→
    16→### Quantitative Limits
    17→- Step description: max 50 words | AC per step: max 5 | Each AC: max 30 words
    18→- Step notes: max 100 words
    19→- Target: small (1-3 steps) = 500, medium (4-8) = 1500, large (9-15) = 3000 tokens
    20→
    21→### Include
    22→WHAT to build (one sentence) | WHY (brief business value) | Observable outcomes (testable AC) | Architectural constraints | Integration points
    23→
    24→### Exclude
    25→HOW to implement | code snippets/class names | algorithm descriptions | step-by-step instructions | tech tutorials | testing strategy (TDD standard) | motivational language | redundancy | examples when description clear
    26→
    27→### Compression Techniques
    28→Bullets over prose | eliminate qualifiers | assume expertise | active voice | omit obvious
    29→
    30→## Acceptance Criteria Abstraction
    31→
    32→AC describe observable outcomes, never internal implementation.
    33→
    34→### Rules
    35→Describe WHAT (behavior) not HOW (implementation) | Never reference private methods (underscore prefix) | Never reference internal class decomposition | Never prescribe signatures/params/return types | Crafter decides structure during GREEN + REFACTOR
    36→
    37→### Good AC Examples
    38→- "DES module importable from installation target after install"
    39→- "DES utility scripts present and executable in scripts directory"
    40→- "Installation uses backup before overwriting existing components"
    41→
    42→### Bad AC Examples (implementation-coupled)
    43→- "_install_des_module() copies src/des/ to ~/.claude/lib/python/des/"
    44→- "verify() checks DES module importable via subprocess test"
    45→- "context.installation_verifier._check_agents() called for verification"
    46→
    47→## Step Decomposition Efficiency
    48→
    49→### Step Ratio Check
    50→Rule: `steps_count / estimated_production_files <= 2.5`
    51→Violation = over-decomposition. Action: merge steps targeting same file.
    52→
    53→### Identical Pattern Detection
    54→Rule: if N steps differ only by substitution variable, batch into 1 step. Threshold: 3+ identical-pattern steps must batch.
    55→Bad: 4 separate steps for AgentsPlugin, CommandsPlugin, TemplatesPlugin, UtilitiesPlugin
    56→Good: 1 step creating all 4
    57→
    58→### No-Op Prevention
    59→Each step must add production code. Validation-only steps are not steps.
    60→Bad: "Step 02-05: Validate extraction with integration tests"
    61→Good: validation is part of preceding step's REVIEW phase
    62→
    63→## Step-to-Scenario Mapping
    64→
    65→For roadmaps feeding DELIVER wave:
    66→1. Read acceptance tests (tests/acceptance/test_*.py) before creating roadmap
    67→2. Count scenarios (def test_*) | 3. ~1 step per scenario (flexibility for infra steps)
    68→4. Mark infra steps: "type: infrastructure" | 5. Principle: 1 Scenario = 1 Step = 1 TDD Cycle
    69→
    70→## Measure Before Plan Gate
    71→
    72→Before any roadmap, verify: 1. Timing data shows WHERE time spent | 2. Impact ranking shows MOST contributing component | 3. Target validation provides evidence
    73→
    74→Missing data: halt, request measurement, offer to help. Gate is blocking.
    75→
    76→## Simplest Solution Gate
    77→
    78→Before multi-phase (>3 steps), document rejected alternatives:
    79→
    80→### Alternatives to Consider
    81→1. Configuration-only (no code) | 2. Single-file change | 3. Existing tool/library | 4. Partial (solve 80% simply)
    82→
    83→### Documentation Format
    84→```markdown
    85→## Rejected Simple Alternatives
    86→
    87→### Alternative 1: {simplest approach}
    88→- What: {description}
    89→- Expected Impact: {% of problem solved}
    90→- Why Insufficient: {evidence-based reason}
    91→
    92→### Why Complex Solution Necessary
    93→1. Simple alternatives fail due to: {specific reason with evidence}
    94→2. Complexity justified by: {benefit simple solutions cannot achieve}
    95→```
    96→
    97→Min 2 alternatives. Each: specific description, expected impact, evidence-based rejection.
    98→
