1→# Architecture Design: Objective & Behavior Nodes
     2→
     3→## System Context
     4→
     5→This feature extends the Brain knowledge graph with two new node types -- **Objective** (strategic goals) and **Behavior** (agent craftsmanship metrics) -- plus the relations and evaluation pipelines that connect them to existing intent authorization, policy enforcement, observer analysis, and learning systems.

    96→
    97→## Integration Points
    98→
    99→### 1. Extraction Pipeline Extension (Objective Extraction)
   100→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/extraction/` processes chat messages, extracts task/decision/question entities
   101→- **Extension**: Add `objective` to extraction entity types. Reuse existing extraction schema pattern with confidence scoring and dedup
   102→- **Dedup**: Semantic similarity check (>0.95) against active workspace objectives before creation
   103→
   104→### 2. Authorizer Extension (Objective Alignment)
   105→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts` runs policy gate then LLM evaluation
   106→- **Extension**: After policy gate passes, run alignment evaluation: KNN query against active objective embeddings. Create `supports` edge on match (>=0.7), warning observation on no match (<0.5 for all)
   107→- **Constraint**: Alignment evaluation must complete within 200ms. Use pre-loaded workspace objective embeddings (cache in memory per scan cycle, or two-step KNN)
   108→
   109→### 3. Policy Gate Extension (Behavior Scores in Context)
   110→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/policy/policy-gate.ts` evaluates rules against `IntentEvaluationContext`
   111→- **Extension**: Enrich `IntentEvaluationContext` with `behavior_scores` object (keyed by metric_type) before policy evaluation. Predicate evaluator already supports dot-path resolution (`behavior_scores.Security_First` with operator `lt` and value `0.8`)
   112→- **No schema change to policy rules needed**: The existing `RulePredicate` type already supports `field: "behavior_scores.Security_First", operator: "lt", value: 0.8` -- the predicate evaluator resolves dot paths
   113→
   114→### 4. Observer Extension (Behavior Telemetry + Coherence)
   115→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts` runs contradiction/stale/drift scans. `learning-diagnosis.ts` clusters observations and proposes learnings
   116→- **Extension A (Telemetry)**: New scan type in graph-scan that queries recent agent sessions and writes behavior records via behavior collector
   117→- **Extension B (Coherence)**: New scan type that detects orphaned decisions (no implementing task after 14d), stale objectives (no supports edges after 14d)
   118→- **Extension C (Behavior Learning)**: New input signal type for learning-diagnosis that feeds behavior trend data (3+ consecutive below-threshold) into existing clustering + root cause pipeline
   119→
   120→### 5. Feed Integration
   121→- **Existing**: SSE events via `app/src/server/streaming/sse-registry.ts`
   122→- **Extension**: New feed card types for alignment warnings, behavior vetos, coherence alerts. Reuse existing observation + suggestion feed card patterns
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Data Flow Diagrams
   127→
   128→### Intent Authorization with Objective Alignment
   129→
   130→```
   131→Intent Submitted
   132→  |
   133→  v
   134→[Policy Gate] -- evaluates rules against context (now includes behavior_scores)
   135→  |
   136→  |-- DENY --> veto (existing flow)
   137→  |
   138→  v (PASS)
   139→[Alignment Evaluator] -- KNN: intent.goal embedding vs active objective embeddings
   140→  |
   141→  |-- similarity >= 0.7 --> create supports edge, proceed
   142→  |-- 0.5 <= similarity < 0.7 --> create supports edge (highest), surface for confirmation
   143→  |-- similarity < 0.5 for all --> create warning observation, proceed (warning mode)
   144→  |-- no objectives exist --> skip alignment, proceed with info feed card
   145→  |
   146→  v
   147→[LLM Evaluator] -- existing flow (unchanged)
   148→  |
   149→  v
   150→Authorization Result
   151→```
   152→
   153→### Behavior Telemetry Pipeline
   154→
   155→```
   156→Agent Session Completes
   157→  |
   158→  v
   159→[Observer Graph Scan] -- queries recent sessions without behavior records
   160→  |
   161→  v
   162→[Behavior Collector] -- for each session:
   163→  |
   164→  |-- extract session telemetry (files_changed, test files, etc.)
   165→  |-- [Behavior Scorer] -- metric-specific normalization (pure function)
   166→  |-- write behavior record to SurrealDB
   167→  |-- create exhibits edge: identity ->exhibits-> behavior
   168→  |
   169→  v
   170→[Behavior Trend Check] -- queries last N behavior records per identity+metric
   171→  |
   172→  |-- 3+ consecutive below threshold --> feed into learning proposal pipeline
   173→  |-- improvement detected after learning --> create positive observation
   174→  |-- no improvement after N sessions --> create warning observation
   175→```
   176→
   177→### Coherence Audit Pipeline
   178→
   179→```
   180→[Observer Graph Scan] -- periodic trigger (extends existing scan)
   181→  |
   182→  +-- [Query Orphaned Decisions] -- confirmed decisions with no implementing task after 14d
   183→  |     |
   184→  |     v create observation (severity: warning)
   185→  |
   186→  +-- [Query Stale Objectives] -- active objectives with no supports edge after 14d
   187→  |     |
   188→  |     v create observation (severity: warning)
   189→  |
   190→  +-- [Query Disconnected Tasks] -- tasks with no project/feature after 14d
   191→  |     |
   192→  |     v create observation (severity: info)
   193→  |
   194→  +-- [Compute Coherence Score] -- connected_nodes / total_nodes
   195→        |
   196→        v store on workspace or emit as feed event
   197→```
   198→
   199→---
   200→
   201→## Quality Attribute Strategies
   202→
   203→### Auditability
   204→- Behavior records are append-only (no UPDATE, no retroactive modification)
   205→- `supports` edges are immutable once created
   206→- Objective status changes logged with timestamp (created_at/updated_at)
   207→- Policy veto overrides create observation with identity reference
   208→- All behavior-driven learnings linked to triggering behavior records via `learning_evidence`
   209→
   210→### Maintainability
   211→- New behavior metric types added by extending `metric_type` enum in schema migration -- no code changes needed beyond scorer function
   212→- Policy behavior rules use existing predicate evaluator with dot-path resolution -- no policy schema changes
   213→- Coherence queries are pure SurrealQL -- new disconnected patterns added as queries, not code
   214→
   215→### Testability
   216→- Behavior scorer is a pure function (input: session telemetry -> output: normalized score)
   217→- Alignment evaluator is a pure function (input: intent embedding + objective embeddings -> output: alignment result)
   218→- Coherence scanner is pure graph queries against test DB
   219→- All existing acceptance test infrastructure (in-process server + isolated DB) applies
   220→
   221→### Performance
   222→- Alignment evaluation: two-step KNN pattern (HNSW index for candidates, then filter by workspace). Pre-limit to 20 candidates
   223→- Behavior score lookup: indexed query on `workspace + identity + metric_type`, ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
   224→- Coherence scan: bounded by LIMIT clauses (50 per entity type), runs in background (not request path)
   225→
   226→### Reliability
   227→- Behavior scoring failure does NOT block agent sessions (fire-and-forget via inflight tracker)
   228→- Alignment failure does NOT block intent authorization in warning mode (fallback: proceed without alignment)
   229→- Coherence audit failures logged as observations (existing pattern from graph-scan.ts error handling)
   230→
   231→---
   232→
   233→## Deployment Architecture
   234→
   235→No changes to deployment topology. Single Bun server process, single SurrealDB instance. New tables and indexes added via versioned migration scripts applied with `bun migrate`.
   236→
   237→Migration sequence:
   238→1. `0032_objective_table.surql` -- objective table + has_objective relation + supports relation + observes extension
   239→2. `0033_behavior_table.surql` -- behavior table + exhibits relation
   240→3. `0034_fulltext_objective.surql` -- fulltext search index for objectives (optional, if entity search needs it)
   241→
   242→---
   243→

   245→
   246→See `docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/technology-stack.md` for rationale.
   247→
   248→## Component Boundaries
   249→
   250→See `docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/component-boundaries.md` for module decomposition.
   251→

1→# Data Models: Objective & Behavior Nodes
     2→
     3→## New Tables
     4→
     5→### `objective` (SCHEMAFULL)
     6→
     7→Strategic goal node. Created by humans via chat extraction or direct creation.
     8→
     9→```sql
    10→DEFINE TABLE objective SCHEMAFULL;
    11→
    12→-- Core fields
    13→DEFINE FIELD title ON objective TYPE string;
    14→DEFINE FIELD description ON objective TYPE option<string>;
    15→DEFINE FIELD target_date ON objective TYPE option<datetime>;
    16→DEFINE FIELD priority ON objective TYPE int
    17→  ASSERT $value >= 0 AND $value <= 100;
    18→DEFINE FIELD status ON objective TYPE string
    19→  ASSERT $value IN ["active", "expired", "completed", "archived"];
    20→
    21→-- Success criteria (array of KPI objects)
    22→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria ON objective TYPE option<array<object>>;
    23→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].metric_name ON objective TYPE string;
    24→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].target_value ON objective TYPE float;
    25→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].current_value ON objective TYPE float;
    26→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].unit ON objective TYPE string;
    27→
    28→-- Provenance
    29→DEFINE FIELD workspace ON objective TYPE record<workspace>;
    30→DEFINE FIELD source_message ON objective TYPE option<record<message>>;
    31→DEFINE FIELD created_by ON objective TYPE option<record<identity>>;
    32→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON objective TYPE datetime;
    33→DEFINE FIELD updated_at ON objective TYPE option<datetime>;
    34→
    35→-- Embedding for semantic matching
    36→DEFINE FIELD embedding ON objective TYPE option<array<float>>;
    37→
    38→-- Indexes
    39→DEFINE INDEX objective_workspace_status ON objective FIELDS workspace, status;
    40→DEFINE INDEX objective_target_date ON objective FIELDS target_date;
    41→DEFINE INDEX idx_objective_embedding ON objective FIELDS embedding HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE;
    42→```
    43→
    44→**Design rationale:**
    45→- `success_criteria` as array of objects matches the KPI model from user stories. Each KPI has metric_name, target_value, current_value, unit -- sufficient for progress tracking without a separate KPI table
    46→- `priority` as 0-100 int matches the intent priority range for consistency
    47→- `status` enum covers the full lifecycle: active -> expired (target_date passed) | completed (human confirmed) | archived (retired)
    48→- No `null` values per project convention -- omitted optional fields use `option<T>` which defaults to `NONE`
    49→
    50→### `behavior` (SCHEMAFULL)
    51→
    52→Agent craftsmanship metric record. Append-only -- one per agent session per metric type.
    53→
    54→```sql
    55→DEFINE TABLE behavior SCHEMAFULL;
    56→
    57→-- Core fields
    58→DEFINE FIELD metric_type ON behavior TYPE string
    59→  ASSERT $value IN [
    60→    "tdd_adherence",
    61→    "security_first",
    62→    "conciseness",
    63→    "review_responsiveness",
    64→    "documentation_quality"
    65→  ];
    66→DEFINE FIELD score ON behavior TYPE float
    67→  ASSERT $value >= 0.0 AND $value <= 1.0;
    68→
    69→-- Telemetry source data (metric-specific, flexible schema)
    70→DEFINE FIELD source_telemetry ON behavior TYPE object FLEXIBLE;
    71→
    72→-- Context
    73→DEFINE FIELD workspace ON behavior TYPE record<workspace>;
    74→DEFINE FIELD session ON behavior TYPE record<agent_session>;
    75→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON behavior TYPE datetime;
    76→
    77→-- Indexes
    78→DEFINE INDEX behavior_workspace_metric ON behavior FIELDS workspace, metric_type;
    79→DEFINE INDEX behavior_session ON behavior FIELDS session;
    80→DEFINE INDEX behavior_created_at ON behavior FIELDS created_at;
    81→```
    82→
    83→**Design rationale:**
    84→- `metric_type` as string enum is extensible via schema migration (add to ASSERT list). No code changes needed to add new metric types beyond the scorer function
    85→- `source_telemetry` as `object FLEXIBLE` allows metric-specific data shapes (TDD: files_changed, test_files_changed; Security: cve_advisories, addressed_count) without requiring DEFINE FIELD for every sub-field
    86→- `session` links to agent_session for provenance (which agent, which workspace, which project)
    87→- No `identity` field on behavior itself -- the identity is resolved via `exhibits` edge or via `session -> agent_session.workspace + identity lookup`
    88→- Append-only enforced at application layer (no UPDATE queries for behavior records)
    89→
    90→### Metric Type Extensibility
    91→
    92→Adding a new behavior metric:
    93→1. Add value to `metric_type` ASSERT list in schema migration
    94→2. Add scorer function in `behavior/scorer.ts` (pure function mapping telemetry to 0-1 score)
    95→3. Add telemetry extraction logic in Observer behavior collector
    96→
    97→No policy schema changes needed -- existing `RulePredicate` with `field: "behavior_scores.<new_metric>"` works immediately.
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## New Relations
   102→
   103→### `supports` (TYPE RELATION IN intent OUT objective)
   104→
   105→Links an intent to the strategic objective it serves. Created by alignment evaluator during intent authorization.
   106→
   107→```sql
   108→DEFINE TABLE supports TYPE RELATION IN intent OUT objective SCHEMAFULL;
   109→DEFINE FIELD alignment_score ON supports TYPE float
   110→  ASSERT $value >= 0.0 AND $value <= 1.0;
   111→DEFINE FIELD alignment_method ON supports TYPE string
   112→  ASSERT $value IN ["automatic", "manual"];
   113→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON supports TYPE datetime;
   114→DEFINE INDEX supports_out ON supports FIELDS out;
   115→```
   116→
   117→**Design rationale:**
   118→- `alignment_score` records the cosine similarity at time of linking (audit trail)
   119→- `alignment_method` distinguishes automatic (Authorizer) from manual (human via feed card) links
   120→- Index on `out` (objective) for efficient "all intents supporting this objective" queries
   121→- Immutable once created (no UPDATE) -- if reassigned, a new edge is created and old one stays for history
   122→
   123→### `exhibits` (TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT behavior)
   124→
   125→Links an agent identity to a behavior record.
   126→
   127→```sql
   128→DEFINE TABLE exhibits TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT behavior SCHEMAFULL;
   129→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON exhibits TYPE datetime;
   130→```
   131→
   132→**Design rationale:**
   133→- Simple edge with timestamp. All rich data lives on the `behavior` node itself
   134→- IN is `identity` (not agent_session) because behavior trends are tracked per-agent-identity across sessions
   135→- Query pattern: `SELECT ->exhibits->behavior FROM identity:x WHERE metric_type = "tdd_adherence" ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`
   136→
   137→---
   138→
   139→### `has_objective` (TYPE RELATION IN project | workspace OUT objective)
   140→
   141→Links a project or workspace to its objectives. Follows the existing `has_project` (workspace->project) and `has_feature` (project->feature) pattern.
   142→
   143→```sql
   144→DEFINE TABLE has_objective TYPE RELATION IN project | workspace OUT objective SCHEMAFULL;
   145→DEFINE FIELD added_at ON has_objective TYPE datetime;
   146→```
   147→
   148→**Design rationale:**
   149→- Objectives are top-level strategic containers (intents support them), not sub-entities. They don't "belong to" something -- they are targets that other work serves
   150→- `has_project` and `has_feature` establish the precedent: container `has` child via dedicated relation
   151→- IN includes both `project` and `workspace` because some objectives are project-specific while others span the entire workspace
   152→- The `objective.workspace` field provides workspace scoping for queries; `has_objective` provides the optional project linkage for graph traversal
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## Extended Relations
   157→
   158→### `observes` extension
   159→
   160→The existing `observes` relation needs OUT type extended to include `objective` and `behavior`:
   161→
   162→```sql
   163→-- Current: IN observation OUT project | feature | task | decision | question | intent | git_commit | observation
   164→-- Extended: add objective | behavior to OUT union
   165→```
   166→
   167→This allows coherence audit observations to link to the objective or behavior they concern.
   168→
   169→### `learning_evidence` extension
   170→
   171→The existing `learning_evidence` relation needs OUT type extended to include `behavior`:
   172→
   173→```sql
   174→-- Current: IN learning OUT message | trace | observation | agent_session
   175→-- Extended: add behavior to OUT union
   176→```
   177→
   178→This allows behavior-driven learnings to reference the triggering behavior records as evidence.
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→## Key Query Patterns
   183→
   184→### Latest behavior score per identity + metric
   185→
   186→```sql
   187→-- Used by policy gate to enrich IntentEvaluationContext
   188→SELECT score, created_at FROM behavior
   189→WHERE session IN (
   190→  SELECT VALUE id FROM agent_session
   191→  WHERE workspace = $ws
   192→  AND ->member_of->identity CONTAINS $identity
   193→)
   194→AND metric_type = $metric
   195→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   196→LIMIT 1;
   197→```
   198→
   199→Alternative (via exhibits edge):
   200→```sql
   201→SELECT <-exhibits<-identity, score, created_at FROM behavior
   202→WHERE workspace = $ws
   203→AND metric_type = $metric
   204→AND id IN (SELECT VALUE out FROM exhibits WHERE `in` = $identity)
   205→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   206→LIMIT 1;
   207→```
   208→
   209→### Objective alignment (KNN)
   210→
   211→```sql
   212→-- Two-step KNN pattern per SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug
   213→LET $candidates = SELECT id, title, embedding,
   214→  vector::similarity::cosine(embedding, $intent_embedding) AS similarity
   215→FROM objective WHERE embedding <|10, COSINE|> $intent_embedding;
   216→
   217→SELECT id, title, similarity FROM $candidates
   218→WHERE workspace = $ws AND status = "active"
   219→ORDER BY similarity DESC
   220→LIMIT 5;
   221→```
   222→
   223→### Coherence: orphaned decisions
   224→
   225→```sql
   226→SELECT id, summary, created_at FROM decision
   227→WHERE workspace = $ws
   228→AND status = "confirmed"
   229→AND created_at < $threshold_date
   230→AND array::len(<-implemented_by<-git_commit) = 0
   231→AND array::len(<-implemented_by<-pull_request) = 0
   232→ORDER BY created_at ASC
   233→LIMIT 50;
   234→```
   235→
   236→### Coherence: stale objectives
   237→
   238→```sql
   239→SELECT id, title, created_at FROM objective
   240→WHERE workspace = $ws
   241→AND status = "active"
   242→AND created_at < $threshold_date
   243→AND array::len(<-supports<-intent) = 0
   244→ORDER BY created_at ASC
   245→LIMIT 50;
   246→```
   247→
   248→### Behavior trend (last N sessions)
   249→
   250→```sql
   251→SELECT score, created_at FROM behavior
   252→WHERE workspace = $ws
   253→AND metric_type = $metric
   254→AND id IN (SELECT VALUE out FROM exhibits WHERE `in` = $identity)
   255→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   256→LIMIT $n;
   257→```
   258→
   259→---
   260→
   261→## Migration Plan
   262→
   263→Three migrations, applied sequentially via `bun migrate`:
   264→
   265→| Migration | Contents |
   266→|-----------|----------|
   267→| `0032_objective_table.surql` | `objective` table, `supports` relation, `has_objective` relation, extend `observes` OUT |
   268→| `0033_behavior_table.surql` | `behavior` table, `exhibits` relation, extend `learning_evidence` OUT |
   269→| `0034_objective_fulltext.surql` | Fulltext search index for objective title (BM25, reuses existing `entity_search` analyzer) |
   270→
   271→Each migration wrapped in `BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;` per project convention.
   272→

1→# Component Boundaries: Objective & Behavior Nodes
     2→
     3→## Module Decomposition
     4→
     5→All new modules follow the existing project convention: pure query/logic functions at the module level, IO at boundaries only. Functional paradigm -- composition pipelines, types-first, pure core / effect shell.
     6→
     7→### New Modules
     8→
     9→```
    10→app/src/server/
    11→  objective/
    12→    queries.ts          -- SurrealDB CRUD for objective table (create, update, list, get)
    13→    alignment.ts        -- Pure: cosine similarity evaluation, alignment result classification
    14→    objective-route.ts  -- HTTP routes for objective CRUD + progress view
    15→  behavior/
    16→    queries.ts          -- SurrealDB CRUD for behavior + exhibits edge
    17→    scorer.ts           -- Pure: metric-specific score normalization functions
    18→    trends.ts           -- Pure: trend computation from behavior record arrays
    19→    behavior-route.ts   -- HTTP routes for behavior scores + trends
    20→```
    21→
    22→### Extended Modules (existing files, new functions)
    23→
    24→```
    25→app/src/server/
    26→  intent/
    27→    authorizer.ts       -- Add alignment evaluation step after policy gate
    28→  policy/
    29→    types.ts            -- Extend IntentEvaluationContext with behavior_scores
    30→  observer/
    31→    graph-scan.ts       -- Add behavior telemetry scan + coherence audit scan types
    32→    learning-diagnosis.ts -- Add behavior trend input signal alongside observation clusters
    33→  extraction/
    34→    schema.ts           -- Add "objective" to extraction entity type enum
    35→```
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Dependency Direction
    40→
    41→```
    42→                    ┌─────────────────────────┐
    43→                    │   HTTP Routes (shell)    │
    44→                    │ objective-route.ts       │
    45→                    │ behavior-route.ts        │
    46→                    └────────┬────────────────┘
    47→                             │ depends on
    48→                    ┌────────▼────────────────┐
    49→                    │   Domain Logic (pure)    │
    50→                    │ alignment.ts             │
    51→                    │ scorer.ts                │
    52→                    │ trends.ts                │
    53→                    └────────┬────────────────┘
    54→                             │ depends on
    55→                    ┌────────▼────────────────┐
    56→                    │   Data Access (effect)   │
    57→                    │ objective/queries.ts     │
    58→                    │ behavior/queries.ts      │
    59→                    └────────┬────────────────┘
    60→                             │ depends on
    61→                    ┌────────▼────────────────┐
    62→                    │   SurrealDB (infra)      │
    63→                    └─────────────────────────┘
    64→```
    65→
    66→Dependencies point inward. Pure domain logic has no IO imports. Route handlers compose pure functions with data access at the boundary.
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Integration Contracts
    71→
    72→### Authorizer -> Alignment Evaluator
    73→- Input: intent goal embedding (number[]), workspace record
    74→- Output: `AlignmentResult = { matched: boolean; objectiveId?: RecordId; score: number; ambiguous: boolean }`
    75→- Contract: returns within 200ms. Pure cosine similarity + KNN query
    76→
    77→### Policy Gate -> Behavior Query
    78→- Input: identity record, workspace record, metric_types (string[])
    79→- Output: `Record<string, number>` (metric_type -> latest score)
    80→- Contract: enriches `IntentEvaluationContext.behavior_scores` before rule evaluation
    81→
    82→### Observer -> Behavior Collector
    83→- Input: agent_session record with files_changed, telemetry
    84→- Output: behavior records written to DB, exhibits edges created
    85→- Contract: fire-and-forget, failure does not propagate
    86→
    87→### Observer -> Coherence Scanner
    88→- Input: workspace record
    89→- Output: observations created for disconnected patterns
    90→- Contract: extends existing `GraphScanResult` type with coherence counts
    91→
    92→### Observer -> Behavior Learning Bridge
    93→- Input: behavior trend data (identity, metric_type, recent scores)
    94→- Output: learning proposals via existing `suggestLearning()` function
    95→- Contract: uses existing learning API, collision detection, dual-gate safety
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## Boundary Rules
   100→
   101→1. **objective/** and **behavior/** modules do NOT import from each other. They are independent subsystems connected only through the Observer (which reads both) and the Authorizer (which reads objectives and behavior scores independently)
   102→
   103→2. **Pure modules** (alignment.ts, scorer.ts, trends.ts) have ZERO imports from `surrealdb`, `ai`, or any IO library. They receive data, return data
   104→
   105→3. **Query modules** (queries.ts) accept `Surreal` and `RecordId` parameters. They do NOT import route-level types or HTTP types
   106→
   107→4. **Route modules** compose queries + pure logic. They are the only modules that import from `../http/` utilities
   108→
   109→5. **Observer extensions** follow existing graph-scan.ts pattern: pure query functions + pipeline composition. New scan types are added as functions called from the existing `runGraphScan` orchestrator
   110→

roadmap:
  project_id: objective-behavior
  created_at: '2026-03-13T19:13:05Z'
  total_steps: 10
  phases: 4
phases:
- id: '01'
  name: 'Graph Layer Foundation'
  steps:
  - id: 01-01
    name: 'Objective schema + CRUD queries'
    description: >
      Create SurrealDB migrations for objective table, has_objective relation, supports relation,
      and observes OUT extension. Implement objective CRUD query functions (create, get, list, update status).
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: []
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-01 #1: Objective created with title, priority, status, workspace scope and persisted to DB'
      - 'US-OB-01 #2: Objective stores multiple success criteria as key result objects'
      - 'US-OB-01 #4: Objective without target_date persists with field omitted (not null)'
      - 'US-OB-01 #5: Objectives scoped to workspace -- query for workspace A returns zero results from workspace B'
      - 'US-OB-01 #3: List query returns all active objectives within a workspace'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Migration 0032 wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION'
      - 'All fields use option<T> for absence, never null'
      - 'Query functions accept Surreal + RecordId params, return typed results'
      - 'HNSW index on embedding field (DIMENSION 1536, DIST COSINE)'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - schema/migrations/0032_objective_table.surql
        - app/src/server/objective/queries.ts
      modified_files:
        - schema/surreal-schema.surql
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-crud.test.ts

  - id: 01-02
    name: 'Behavior telemetry collection + scorer'
    description: >
      Create behavior table migration with exhibits relation and learning_evidence OUT extension.
      Implement behavior CRUD queries, pure scorer functions for metric normalization, and exhibits edge writer.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: []
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-03 #1: Behavior record created with metric_type, score (0-1), source_telemetry; exhibits edge links identity to behavior'
      - 'US-OB-03 #2: Multiple metric types per agent (tdd_adherence + security_first) each produce separate records'
      - 'US-OB-03 #4: New session creates new behavior record (append-only, no UPDATE)'
      - 'US-OB-03 #5: Agent with no sessions returns empty behavior array'
      - 'US-OB-03 #6: Score boundary values 0.0 and 1.0 accepted by ASSERT constraint'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Migration 0033 wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT TRANSACTION'
      - 'scorer.ts is pure -- zero IO imports, maps telemetry to 0-1 float'
      - 'source_telemetry uses TYPE object FLEXIBLE for metric-specific shapes'
      - 'Behavior records are append-only at application layer'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - schema/migrations/0033_behavior_table.surql
        - app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts
        - app/src/server/behavior/scorer.ts
      modified_files:
        - schema/surreal-schema.surql
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-telemetry.test.ts

  - id: 01-03
    name: 'Objective + behavior HTTP routes'
    description: >
      Expose objective CRUD and behavior score/trend queries via REST endpoints.
      Register routes in start-server.ts. Create fulltext search index migration for objective title.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-01, 01-02]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-01 #3: GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/objectives returns workspace-scoped objective list'
      - 'US-OB-03 #3: Behavior trend visible across 5 sessions via GET endpoint'
      - 'US-OB-03 #8: Behavior records returned are workspace-scoped'
      - 'POST creates objective, GET retrieves by ID, PUT updates status'
      - 'Behavior scores queryable by identity and metric_type'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Migration 0034 for fulltext index uses existing entity_search analyzer with BM25(1.2, 0.75)'
      - 'Route modules compose queries + pure logic at boundary only'
      - 'Routes registered in start-server.ts following existing pattern'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - schema/migrations/0034_objective_fulltext.surql
        - app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts
        - app/src/server/behavior/behavior-route.ts
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-crud.test.ts
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-telemetry.test.ts

- id: '02'
  name: 'Alignment and Policy'
  steps:
  - id: 02-01
    name: 'Intent-objective alignment evaluator'
    description: >
      Pure cosine similarity function classifying alignment result (matched/ambiguous/none).
      Supports edge writer creates intent->supports->objective edges. Integrate into authorizer
      after policy gate pass.
    time_estimate: 4h
    dependencies: [01-01]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-02 #1: Supports edge created with alignment_score when intent cosine similarity >= 0.7 to an active objective'
      - 'US-OB-02 #3: Manual supports edge records alignment_method as "manual"'
      - 'US-OB-02 #6: Multiple intents can support the same objective via separate supports edges'
      - 'Alignment failure does not block intent authorization (warning mode fallback)'
      - 'Two-step KNN pattern used for HNSW+workspace filter'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'alignment.ts is pure -- receives embeddings, returns AlignmentResult'
      - 'Supports edges are immutable once created'
      - 'KNN query uses two-step pattern per SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug'
      - 'Alignment step added after policy gate in authorizer pipeline'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - app/src/server/objective/alignment.ts
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/intent-alignment.test.ts

  - id: 02-02
    name: 'Behavior-based policy enforcement'
    description: >
      Enrich IntentEvaluationContext with behavior_scores before policy rule evaluation.
      Query latest score per identity+metric_type. Existing predicate evaluator resolves
      dot-path fields (behavior_scores.tdd_adherence) without schema changes.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-02]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-04 #1: Intent vetoed when behavior score below policy threshold (e.g., tdd_adherence < 0.6)'
      - 'US-OB-04 #2: Intent proceeds when all behavior scores above respective thresholds'
      - 'US-OB-04 #3: Testing-mode policy logs veto reason without blocking authorization'
      - 'US-OB-04 #5: Agent with no behavior data passes policy (no score = no veto)'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'No policy schema changes -- existing RulePredicate with dot-path resolution handles behavior_scores.*'
      - 'Behavior score lookup indexed on workspace + metric_type, ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1'
      - 'Context enrichment happens before policy gate evaluation, not inside it'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/policy/types.ts
        - app/src/server/behavior/queries.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-policy.test.ts

- id: '03'
  name: 'Visibility and Auditing'
  steps:
  - id: 03-01
    name: 'Objective progress visibility'
    description: >
      Compute supporting intent count per objective via supports edges.
      Track success criteria current_value vs target_value. Detect expired objectives by target_date.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [02-01]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-05 #1: Objective progress shows correct count of supporting intents'
      - 'US-OB-05 #2: Success criteria display current_value and target_value per KPI'
      - 'US-OB-05 #3: Objective with zero supporting intents identifiable as unsupported'
      - 'US-OB-05 #4: Objective past target_date detected as expired'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Progress computed via graph traversal (count <-supports<-intent), not denormalized counter'
      - 'Expiration is query-time check, not background job'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/objective/queries.ts
        - app/src/server/objective/objective-route.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/objective-progress.test.ts

  - id: 03-02
    name: 'Coherence auditor scans'
    description: >
      Extend observer graph-scan with coherence detection: orphaned decisions (confirmed, no implementing
      task/commit after 14d), stale objectives (active, no supports edges after 14d). Create observations
      for disconnected patterns.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-01]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-06 #1: Orphaned decision (confirmed, 14d+ old, no implementing task) detected and observation created'
      - 'US-OB-06 #2: Stale objective (active, 14d+ old, no supports edges) detected and observation created'
      - 'US-OB-06 #3: Connected objective with supports edges not flagged as stale'
      - 'US-OB-06 #5: Recently created decision (< 14d) not flagged as orphan regardless of implementation status'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Coherence queries are pure SurrealQL with LIMIT 50 per entity type'
      - 'New scan types added as functions called from existing runGraphScan orchestrator'
      - 'Observations use existing observes relation (extended OUT includes objective)'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/coherence-auditor.test.ts

  - id: 03-03
    name: 'Behavior trend computation'
    description: >
      Pure trend analysis functions: detect consecutive below-threshold streaks, improvement
      after learning injection, flat-line patterns. Operates on behavior record arrays with no DB access.
    time_estimate: 2h
    dependencies: [01-02]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-07 #1: 3+ consecutive below-threshold scores detected as drift pattern'
      - 'US-OB-07 #2: Improving scores after learning injection detectable as positive trend'
      - 'US-OB-07 #4: Flat scores after learning detected as ineffective'
      - 'US-OB-07 #6: Single session score does not trigger drift detection (requires 3+ data points)'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'trends.ts is pure -- zero IO imports, receives score arrays, returns trend classification'
      - 'Threshold and streak length are parameters, not hardcoded'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files:
        - app/src/server/behavior/trends.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-learning.test.ts

- id: '04'
  name: 'Learning Loop'
  steps:
  - id: 04-01
    name: 'Behavior learning bridge'
    description: >
      Feed behavior trend signals into existing learning proposal pipeline. Observer queries
      recent behavior records, runs trend analysis, proposes learnings with behavior evidence.
      Rate-limited to 5 proposals per agent per 7 days.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [03-03]
    criteria:
      - 'US-OB-07 #3: Learning proposal contains correct observer metadata (source_agent, metric_type, evidence links)'
      - 'US-OB-07 #5: Rate limit prevents more than 5 proposals per agent identity per 7 days'
      - 'Learning proposals use existing suggestLearning() with collision detection'
      - 'Behavior records linked as learning_evidence (via extended relation OUT)'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Uses existing learning API and collision detection -- no new learning creation path'
      - 'Behavior trend input alongside observation clusters in learning-diagnosis pipeline'
      - 'Fire-and-forget via inflight tracker -- failure does not block observer scan'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts
        - app/src/server/observer/learning-diagnosis.ts
      test_files:
        - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/behavior-learning.test.ts

  - id: 04-02
    name: 'Graph UI visualization for objective + behavior nodes'
    description: >
      Extend existing graph visualization components with objective and behavior node rendering,
      supports/exhibits/has_objective edge display. Leverage partially-implemented graph UI from
      commit f71e3c00.
    time_estimate: 3h
    dependencies: [01-03]
    criteria:
      - 'Objective nodes render in graph view with title, status, priority'
      - 'Behavior nodes render with metric_type and score'
      - 'Supports edges visible between intent and objective nodes'
      - 'Exhibits edges visible between identity and behavior nodes'
    architectural_constraints:
      - 'Extend existing Reagraph graph components -- no new visualization library'
      - 'Node type colors/shapes follow existing graph UI conventions'
      - 'Graph data fetched from existing GET /api/graph/:workspaceId endpoint (extended)'
    implementation_scope:
      new_files: []
      modified_files:
        - app/src/client/
      test_files: []

implementation_scope:
  source_directories:
    - app/src/server/objective/
    - app/src/server/behavior/
    - app/src/client/
  test_directories:
    - tests/acceptance/objective-behavior/
  excluded_patterns:
    - node_modules/**
    - dist/**
validation:
  status: pending
  reviewer: solution-architect-reviewer
  approved_at: pending

content.text:
<!-- DES-ENFORCEMENT : exempt -->

Review the roadmap at docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml for quality, completeness, and feasibility.

## Review Scope

Evaluate against these criteria:
1. **Completeness**: Do all steps cover the 7 user stories (US-OB-01 through US-OB-07) from the test scenarios?
2. **Acceptance criteria quality**: Are ACs behavioral and measurable? Do they reference specific test scenario IDs?
3. **Dependency correctness**: Do step dependencies match the walking skeleton dependency graph?
4. **Architectural constraints**: Are they consistent with CLAUDE.md conventions (functional paradigm, no null, SCHEMAFULL, RecordId, etc.)?
5. **Implementation scope accuracy**: Do file paths match the component-boundaries.md module decomposition?
6. **Step atomicity**: Is each step self-contained and testable independently?
7. **Risk assessment**: Any steps that seem too large, too vague, or missing key considerations?

## Context Files

Read these for review context:
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/roadmap.yaml (the artifact under review)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/walking-skeleton.md (implementation sequence)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/distill/test-scenarios.md (all 47 test scenarios)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/component-boundaries.md (module decomposition)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/data-models.md (schema design)
- docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/architecture-design.md (full architecture)
- CLAUDE.md (project conventions)

## Output Format

Provide:
1. APPROVE or REJECT (with specific blocking issues)
2. List of findings: CRITICAL (must fix) / HIGH (should fix) / LOW (nice to have)
3. If APPROVE: note any non-blocking improvements for implementation phase

TASK BOUNDARY: Review only. Do NOT modify any files. Return your verdict to the orchestrator.

1→# Walking Skeleton: Objective & Behavior Nodes
     2→
     3→Implementation order using the one-at-a-time strategy. Each skeleton proves a user can accomplish a goal through the driving port. Enable one, implement until green, commit, repeat.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Implementation Sequence
     8→
     9→### Phase 1: Graph Layer Foundation
    10→
    11→**Skeleton 1: Leader creates a strategic objective** (US-OB-01)
    12→- File: `objective-crud.test.ts`
    13→- Proves: Objective node created with all fields, workspace-scoped, persisted
    14→- Driving port: SurrealDB (later: POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/objectives)
    15→- Enable first. All subsequent skeletons depend on objective nodes existing.
    16→
    17→**Skeleton 3: Observer records agent behavior** (US-OB-03)
    18→- File: `behavior-telemetry.test.ts`
    19→- Proves: Behavior record created with metric_type, score, source_telemetry; exhibits edge links identity to behavior
    20→- Driving port: SurrealDB (later: Observer Agent session evaluation)
    21→- Independent of Skeleton 1. Can be implemented in parallel.
    22→
    23→### Phase 2: Alignment and Policy
    24→
    25→**Skeleton 2: Intent aligned with active objective** (US-OB-02)
    26→- File: `intent-alignment.test.ts`
    27→- Proves: Supports edge created between intent and objective with alignment score
    28→- Driving port: Intent authorization pipeline (simulated via DB + supports edge creation)
    29→- Depends on: Skeleton 1 (objectives must exist)
    30→
    31→**Skeleton 4: Behavior policy vetoes deploy intent** (US-OB-04)
    32→- File: `behavior-policy.test.ts`
    33→- Proves: Policy queries behavior score, score below threshold vetoes intent
    34→- Driving port: Policy evaluation (simulated via DB queries + intent status update)
    35→- Depends on: Skeleton 3 (behavior records must exist)
    36→
    37→### Phase 3: Visibility and Auditing
    38→
    39→**Skeleton 5: Objective progress from supporting intents** (US-OB-05)
    40→- File: `objective-progress.test.ts`
    41→- Proves: Supporting intent count computed, success criteria tracked
    42→- Driving port: GET /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/objectives/:id
    43→- Depends on: Skeleton 1 + Skeleton 2 (objectives and supports edges)
    44→
    45→**Skeleton 6: Coherence auditor detects disconnected patterns** (US-OB-06)
    46→- File: `coherence-auditor.test.ts`
    47→- Proves: Orphaned decisions and stale objectives are detectable via graph queries
    48→- Driving port: POST /api/observe/scan/:workspaceId
    49→- Depends on: Skeleton 1 (objectives for stale detection)
    50→
    51→### Phase 4: Learning Loop
    52→
    53→**Skeleton 7: Observer proposes learning from behavior pattern** (US-OB-07)
    54→- File: `behavior-learning.test.ts`
    55→- Proves: 3+ consecutive below-threshold sessions produce detectable drift; learning proposal has correct metadata
    56→- Driving port: Observer graph scan + POST /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/learnings
    57→- Depends on: Skeleton 3 (behavior records for trend analysis)
    58→
    59→---

    65→1. Enable next focused scenario in the same file
    66→2. Implement production code to make it pass

    68→4. Repeat until all scenarios in the file are green

1→# Component Boundaries: Objective & Behavior Nodes
     2→
     3→## Module Decomposition
     4→
     5→All new modules follow the existing project convention: pure query/logic functions at the module level, IO at boundaries only. Functional paradigm -- composition pipelines, types-first, pure core / effect shell.
     6→
     7→### New Modules
     8→
     9→```
    10→app/src/server/
    11→  objective/
    12→    queries.ts          -- SurrealDB CRUD for objective table (create, update, list, get)
    13→    alignment.ts        -- Pure: cosine similarity evaluation, alignment result classification
    14→    objective-route.ts  -- HTTP routes for objective CRUD + progress view
    15→  behavior/
    16→    queries.ts          -- SurrealDB CRUD for behavior + exhibits edge
    17→    scorer.ts           -- Pure: metric-specific score normalization functions
    18→    trends.ts           -- Pure: trend computation from behavior record arrays
    19→    behavior-route.ts   -- HTTP routes for behavior scores + trends
    20→```
    21→
    22→### Extended Modules (existing files, new functions)
    23→
    24→```
    25→app/src/server/
    26→  intent/
    27→    authorizer.ts       -- Add alignment evaluation step after policy gate
    28→  policy/
    29→    types.ts            -- Extend IntentEvaluationContext with behavior_scores
    30→  observer/
    31→    graph-scan.ts       -- Add behavior telemetry scan + coherence audit scan types
    32→    learning-diagnosis.ts -- Add behavior trend input signal alongside observation clusters
    33→  extraction/
    34→    schema.ts           -- Add "objective" to extraction entity type enum
    35→```
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Dependency Direction
    40→
    41→```
    42→                    ┌─────────────────────────┐
    43→                    │   HTTP Routes (shell)    │
    44→                    │ objective-route.ts       │
    45→                    │ behavior-route.ts        │
    46→                    └────────┬────────────────┘
    47→                             │ depends on
    48→                    ┌────────▼────────────────┐
    49→                    │   Domain Logic (pure)    │
    50→                    │ alignment.ts             │
    51→                    │ scorer.ts                │
    52→                    │ trends.ts                │
    53→                    └────────┬────────────────┘
    54→                             │ depends on
    55→                    ┌────────▼────────────────┐
    56→                    │   Data Access (effect)   │
    57→                    │ objective/queries.ts     │
    58→                    │ behavior/queries.ts      │
    59→                    └────────┬────────────────┘
    60→                             │ depends on
    61→                    ┌────────▼────────────────┐
    62→                    │   SurrealDB (infra)      │
    63→                    └─────────────────────────┘
    64→```
    65→
    66→Dependencies point inward. Pure domain logic has no IO imports. Route handlers compose pure functions with data access at the boundary.
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Integration Contracts
    71→
    72→### Authorizer -> Alignment Evaluator
    73→- Input: intent goal embedding (number[]), workspace record
    74→- Output: `AlignmentResult = { matched: boolean; objectiveId?: RecordId; score: number; ambiguous: boolean }`
    75→- Contract: returns within 200ms. Pure cosine similarity + KNN query
    76→
    77→### Policy Gate -> Behavior Query
    78→- Input: identity record, workspace record, metric_types (string[])
    79→- Output: `Record<string, number>` (metric_type -> latest score)
    80→- Contract: enriches `IntentEvaluationContext.behavior_scores` before rule evaluation
    81→
    82→### Observer -> Behavior Collector
    83→- Input: agent_session record with files_changed, telemetry
    84→- Output: behavior records written to DB, exhibits edges created
    85→- Contract: fire-and-forget, failure does not propagate
    86→
    87→### Observer -> Coherence Scanner
    88→- Input: workspace record
    89→- Output: observations created for disconnected patterns
    90→- Contract: extends existing `GraphScanResult` type with coherence counts
    91→
    92→### Observer -> Behavior Learning Bridge
    93→- Input: behavior trend data (identity, metric_type, recent scores)
    94→- Output: learning proposals via existing `suggestLearning()` function
    95→- Contract: uses existing learning API, collision detection, dual-gate safety
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## Boundary Rules
   100→
   101→1. **objective/** and **behavior/** modules do NOT import from each other. They are independent subsystems connected only through the Observer (which reads both) and the Authorizer (which reads objectives and behavior scores independently)
   102→
   103→2. **Pure modules** (alignment.ts, scorer.ts, trends.ts) have ZERO imports from `surrealdb`, `ai`, or any IO library. They receive data, return data
   104→
   105→3. **Query modules** (queries.ts) accept `Surreal` and `RecordId` parameters. They do NOT import route-level types or HTTP types
   106→
   107→4. **Route modules** compose queries + pure logic. They are the only modules that import from `../http/` utilities
   108→
   109→5. **Observer extensions** follow existing graph-scan.ts pattern: pure query functions + pipeline composition. New scan types are added as functions called from the existing `runGraphScan` orchestrator
   110→

1→# Data Models: Objective & Behavior Nodes
     2→
     3→## New Tables
     4→
     5→### `objective` (SCHEMAFULL)
     6→
     7→Strategic goal node. Created by humans via chat extraction or direct creation.
     8→
     9→```sql
    10→DEFINE TABLE objective SCHEMAFULL;
    11→
    12→-- Core fields
    13→DEFINE FIELD title ON objective TYPE string;
    14→DEFINE FIELD description ON objective TYPE option<string>;
    15→DEFINE FIELD target_date ON objective TYPE option<datetime>;
    16→DEFINE FIELD priority ON objective TYPE int
    17→  ASSERT $value >= 0 AND $value <= 100;
    18→DEFINE FIELD status ON objective TYPE string
    19→  ASSERT $value IN ["active", "expired", "completed", "archived"];
    20→
    21→-- Success criteria (array of KPI objects)
    22→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria ON objective TYPE option<array<object>>;
    23→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].metric_name ON objective TYPE string;
    24→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].target_value ON objective TYPE float;
    25→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].current_value ON objective TYPE float;
    26→DEFINE FIELD success_criteria[*].unit ON objective TYPE string;
    27→
    28→-- Provenance
    29→DEFINE FIELD workspace ON objective TYPE record<workspace>;
    30→DEFINE FIELD source_message ON objective TYPE option<record<message>>;
    31→DEFINE FIELD created_by ON objective TYPE option<record<identity>>;
    32→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON objective TYPE datetime;
    33→DEFINE FIELD updated_at ON objective TYPE option<datetime>;
    34→
    35→-- Embedding for semantic matching
    36→DEFINE FIELD embedding ON objective TYPE option<array<float>>;
    37→
    38→-- Indexes
    39→DEFINE INDEX objective_workspace_status ON objective FIELDS workspace, status;
    40→DEFINE INDEX objective_target_date ON objective FIELDS target_date;
    41→DEFINE INDEX idx_objective_embedding ON objective FIELDS embedding HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE;
    42→```
    43→
    44→**Design rationale:**
    45→- `success_criteria` as array of objects matches the KPI model from user stories. Each KPI has metric_name, target_value, current_value, unit -- sufficient for progress tracking without a separate KPI table
    46→- `priority` as 0-100 int matches the intent priority range for consistency
    47→- `status` enum covers the full lifecycle: active -> expired (target_date passed) | completed (human confirmed) | archived (retired)
    48→- No `null` values per project convention -- omitted optional fields use `option<T>` which defaults to `NONE`
    49→
    50→### `behavior` (SCHEMAFULL)
    51→
    52→Agent craftsmanship metric record. Append-only -- one per agent session per metric type.
    53→
    54→```sql
    55→DEFINE TABLE behavior SCHEMAFULL;
    56→
    57→-- Core fields
    58→DEFINE FIELD metric_type ON behavior TYPE string
    59→  ASSERT $value IN [
    60→    "tdd_adherence",
    61→    "security_first",
    62→    "conciseness",
    63→    "review_responsiveness",
    64→    "documentation_quality"
    65→  ];
    66→DEFINE FIELD score ON behavior TYPE float
    67→  ASSERT $value >= 0.0 AND $value <= 1.0;
    68→
    69→-- Telemetry source data (metric-specific, flexible schema)
    70→DEFINE FIELD source_telemetry ON behavior TYPE object FLEXIBLE;
    71→
    72→-- Context
    73→DEFINE FIELD workspace ON behavior TYPE record<workspace>;
    74→DEFINE FIELD session ON behavior TYPE record<agent_session>;
    75→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON behavior TYPE datetime;
    76→
    77→-- Indexes
    78→DEFINE INDEX behavior_workspace_metric ON behavior FIELDS workspace, metric_type;
    79→DEFINE INDEX behavior_session ON behavior FIELDS session;
    80→DEFINE INDEX behavior_created_at ON behavior FIELDS created_at;
    81→```
    82→
    83→**Design rationale:**
    84→- `metric_type` as string enum is extensible via schema migration (add to ASSERT list). No code changes needed to add new metric types beyond the scorer function
    85→- `source_telemetry` as `object FLEXIBLE` allows metric-specific data shapes (TDD: files_changed, test_files_changed; Security: cve_advisories, addressed_count) without requiring DEFINE FIELD for every sub-field
    86→- `session` links to agent_session for provenance (which agent, which workspace, which project)
    87→- No `identity` field on behavior itself -- the identity is resolved via `exhibits` edge or via `session -> agent_session.workspace + identity lookup`
    88→- Append-only enforced at application layer (no UPDATE queries for behavior records)
    89→
    90→### Metric Type Extensibility
    91→
    92→Adding a new behavior metric:
    93→1. Add value to `metric_type` ASSERT list in schema migration
    94→2. Add scorer function in `behavior/scorer.ts` (pure function mapping telemetry to 0-1 score)
    95→3. Add telemetry extraction logic in Observer behavior collector
    96→
    97→No policy schema changes needed -- existing `RulePredicate` with `field: "behavior_scores.<new_metric>"` works immediately.
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## New Relations
   102→
   103→### `supports` (TYPE RELATION IN intent OUT objective)
   104→
   105→Links an intent to the strategic objective it serves. Created by alignment evaluator during intent authorization.
   106→
   107→```sql
   108→DEFINE TABLE supports TYPE RELATION IN intent OUT objective SCHEMAFULL;
   109→DEFINE FIELD alignment_score ON supports TYPE float
   110→  ASSERT $value >= 0.0 AND $value <= 1.0;
   111→DEFINE FIELD alignment_method ON supports TYPE string
   112→  ASSERT $value IN ["automatic", "manual"];
   113→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON supports TYPE datetime;
   114→DEFINE INDEX supports_out ON supports FIELDS out;
   115→```
   116→
   117→**Design rationale:**
   118→- `alignment_score` records the cosine similarity at time of linking (audit trail)
   119→- `alignment_method` distinguishes automatic (Authorizer) from manual (human via feed card) links
   120→- Index on `out` (objective) for efficient "all intents supporting this objective" queries
   121→- Immutable once created (no UPDATE) -- if reassigned, a new edge is created and old one stays for history
   122→
   123→### `exhibits` (TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT behavior)
   124→
   125→Links an agent identity to a behavior record.
   126→
   127→```sql
   128→DEFINE TABLE exhibits TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT behavior SCHEMAFULL;
   129→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON exhibits TYPE datetime;
   130→```
   131→
   132→**Design rationale:**
   133→- Simple edge with timestamp. All rich data lives on the `behavior` node itself
   134→- IN is `identity` (not agent_session) because behavior trends are tracked per-agent-identity across sessions
   135→- Query pattern: `SELECT ->exhibits->behavior FROM identity:x WHERE metric_type = "tdd_adherence" ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`
   136→
   137→---
   138→
   139→### `has_objective` (TYPE RELATION IN project | workspace OUT objective)
   140→
   141→Links a project or workspace to its objectives. Follows the existing `has_project` (workspace->project) and `has_feature` (project->feature) pattern.
   142→
   143→```sql
   144→DEFINE TABLE has_objective TYPE RELATION IN project | workspace OUT objective SCHEMAFULL;
   145→DEFINE FIELD added_at ON has_objective TYPE datetime;
   146→```
   147→
   148→**Design rationale:**
   149→- Objectives are top-level strategic containers (intents support them), not sub-entities. They don't "belong to" something -- they are targets that other work serves
   150→- `has_project` and `has_feature` establish the precedent: container `has` child via dedicated relation
   151→- IN includes both `project` and `workspace` because some objectives are project-specific while others span the entire workspace
   152→- The `objective.workspace` field provides workspace scoping for queries; `has_objective` provides the optional project linkage for graph traversal
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## Extended Relations
   157→
   158→### `observes` extension
   159→
   160→The existing `observes` relation needs OUT type extended to include `objective` and `behavior`:
   161→
   162→```sql
   163→-- Current: IN observation OUT project | feature | task | decision | question | intent | git_commit | observation
   164→-- Extended: add objective | behavior to OUT union
   165→```
   166→
   167→This allows coherence audit observations to link to the objective or behavior they concern.
   168→
   169→### `learning_evidence` extension
   170→
   171→The existing `learning_evidence` relation needs OUT type extended to include `behavior`:
   172→
   173→```sql
   174→-- Current: IN learning OUT message | trace | observation | agent_session
   175→-- Extended: add behavior to OUT union
   176→```
   177→
   178→This allows behavior-driven learnings to reference the triggering behavior records as evidence.
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→## Key Query Patterns
   183→
   184→### Latest behavior score per identity + metric
   185→
   186→```sql
   187→-- Used by policy gate to enrich IntentEvaluationContext
   188→SELECT score, created_at FROM behavior
   189→WHERE session IN (
   190→  SELECT VALUE id FROM agent_session
   191→  WHERE workspace = $ws
   192→  AND ->member_of->identity CONTAINS $identity
   193→)
   194→AND metric_type = $metric
   195→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   196→LIMIT 1;
   197→```
   198→
   199→Alternative (via exhibits edge):
   200→```sql
   201→SELECT <-exhibits<-identity, score, created_at FROM behavior
   202→WHERE workspace = $ws
   203→AND metric_type = $metric
   204→AND id IN (SELECT VALUE out FROM exhibits WHERE `in` = $identity)
   205→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   206→LIMIT 1;
   207→```
   208→
   209→### Objective alignment (KNN)
   210→
   211→```sql
   212→-- Two-step KNN pattern per SurrealDB HNSW+WHERE bug
   213→LET $candidates = SELECT id, title, embedding,
   214→  vector::similarity::cosine(embedding, $intent_embedding) AS similarity
   215→FROM objective WHERE embedding <|10, COSINE|> $intent_embedding;
   216→
   217→SELECT id, title, similarity FROM $candidates
   218→WHERE workspace = $ws AND status = "active"
   219→ORDER BY similarity DESC
   220→LIMIT 5;
   221→```
   222→
   223→### Coherence: orphaned decisions
   224→
   225→```sql
   226→SELECT id, summary, created_at FROM decision
   227→WHERE workspace = $ws
   228→AND status = "confirmed"
   229→AND created_at < $threshold_date
   230→AND array::len(<-implemented_by<-git_commit) = 0
   231→AND array::len(<-implemented_by<-pull_request) = 0
   232→ORDER BY created_at ASC
   233→LIMIT 50;
   234→```
   235→
   236→### Coherence: stale objectives
   237→
   238→```sql
   239→SELECT id, title, created_at FROM objective
   240→WHERE workspace = $ws
   241→AND status = "active"
   242→AND created_at < $threshold_date
   243→AND array::len(<-supports<-intent) = 0
   244→ORDER BY created_at ASC
   245→LIMIT 50;
   246→```
   247→
   248→### Behavior trend (last N sessions)
   249→
   250→```sql
   251→SELECT score, created_at FROM behavior
   252→WHERE workspace = $ws
   253→AND metric_type = $metric
   254→AND id IN (SELECT VALUE out FROM exhibits WHERE `in` = $identity)
   255→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   256→LIMIT $n;
   257→```
   258→
   259→---
   260→
   261→## Migration Plan
   262→
   263→Three migrations, applied sequentially via `bun migrate`:
   264→
   265→| Migration | Contents |
   266→|-----------|----------|
   267→| `0032_objective_table.surql` | `objective` table, `supports` relation, `has_objective` relation, extend `observes` OUT |
   268→| `0033_behavior_table.surql` | `behavior` table, `exhibits` relation, extend `learning_evidence` OUT |
   269→| `0034_objective_fulltext.surql` | Fulltext search index for objective title (BM25, reuses existing `entity_search` analyzer) |
   270→
   271→Each migration wrapped in `BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;` per project convention.
   272→

1→# Architecture Design: Objective & Behavior Nodes
     2→
     3→## System Context
     4→
     5→This feature extends the Brain knowledge graph with two new node types -- **Objective** (strategic goals) and **Behavior** (agent craftsmanship metrics) -- plus the relations and evaluation pipelines that connect them to existing intent authorization, policy enforcement, observer analysis, and learning systems.
     6→
     7→### C4 System Context (L1)
     8→
     9→```mermaid
    10→C4Context
    11→    title System Context: Objective & Behavior Nodes
    12→
    13→    Person(human, "Human Operator", "Creates objectives, reviews behavior, approves learnings")
    14→    System(brain, "Brain Platform", "Knowledge graph with objective alignment, behavior telemetry, and policy enforcement")
    15→    System_Ext(coding_agents, "Coding Agents", "MCP-connected agents submitting intents")
    16→    System_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider", "OpenRouter/Ollama for extraction, evaluation, observer reasoning")
    17→
    18→    Rel(human, brain, "Creates objectives, reviews behavior, approves learnings")
    19→    Rel(coding_agents, brain, "Submits intents, reports session data")
    20→    Rel(brain, llm, "Extraction, alignment evaluation, behavior classification")
    21→    Rel(brain, coding_agents, "Injects learnings via JIT prompt, authorization decisions")
    22→```
    23→
    24→### C4 Container (L2)
    25→
    26→```mermaid
    27→C4Container
    28→    title Container: Objective & Behavior Integration
    29→
    30→    Person(human, "Human Operator")
    31→    System_Ext(agents, "Coding Agents")
    32→
    33→    Container_Boundary(brain, "Brain Platform") {
    34→        Container(web, "Web UI", "React", "Objective progress view, behavior dashboard")
    35→        Container(api, "Bun Server", "TypeScript/Bun", "HTTP API, SSE streaming, agent orchestration")
    36→        ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "Graph DB", "Objectives, behaviors, supports/exhibits edges, policies")
    37→
    38→        Container_Boundary(api_modules, "Server Modules") {
    39→            Component(obj_routes, "Objective Routes", "CRUD, progress, alignment queries")
    40→            Component(beh_routes, "Behavior Routes", "Score queries, trend computation")
    41→            Component(authorizer, "Authorizer", "Intent evaluation + objective alignment")
    42→            Component(policy_gate, "Policy Gate", "Rule evaluation + behavior score lookup")
    43→            Component(observer, "Observer Agent", "Behavior telemetry, coherence audit, learning proposals")
    44→            Component(extraction, "Extraction Pipeline", "Objective extraction from chat")
    45→        }
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    Rel(human, web, "Views objectives, behavior scores, feed cards")
    49→    Rel(web, api, "REST + SSE")
    50→    Rel(agents, api, "Submits intents, session data via MCP")
    51→    Rel(api, surreal, "Graph queries, CRUD")
    52→    Rel(authorizer, surreal, "Reads objective embeddings, writes supports edges")
    53→    Rel(policy_gate, surreal, "Reads latest behavior scores")
    54→    Rel(observer, surreal, "Writes behavior records, reads trends, proposes learnings")
    55→    Rel(extraction, surreal, "Writes objective nodes from chat")
    56→```
    57→
    58→### C4 Component (L3) -- Objective-Behavior Subsystem
    59→
    60→```mermaid
    61→C4Component
    62→    title Component: Objective-Behavior Subsystem
    63→
    64→    Container_Boundary(subsystem, "Objective-Behavior Subsystem") {
    65→        Component(obj_crud, "Objective CRUD", "Create, read, update objective nodes")
    66→        Component(obj_extract, "Objective Extractor", "Extends extraction pipeline for objective entity type")
    67→        Component(alignment_eval, "Alignment Evaluator", "Cosine similarity: intent.goal vs objective embeddings")
    68→        Component(supports_writer, "Supports Edge Writer", "Creates intent->supports->objective edges")
    69→        Component(beh_collector, "Behavior Collector", "Evaluates session telemetry, writes behavior records")
    70→        Component(beh_scorer, "Behavior Scorer", "Metric-specific score normalization (pure)")
    71→        Component(beh_query, "Behavior Query", "Latest score, trend computation, per-identity aggregation")
    72→        Component(coherence_scan, "Coherence Scanner", "Graph queries for disconnected patterns")
    73→        Component(beh_learning, "Behavior Learning Bridge", "Feeds behavior trends into existing learning proposal pipeline")
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    Container_Boundary(existing, "Existing Infrastructure (unchanged)") {
    77→        Component(policy_gate, "Policy Gate", "Evaluates rules against IntentEvaluationContext")
    78→        Component(predicate_eval, "Predicate Evaluator", "Resolves dot-path fields, applies operators")
    79→        Component(learning_api, "Learning API", "CRUD + collision detection + JIT injection")
    80→        Component(observer_diag, "Observer Diagnostics", "Clustering, root cause, learning proposals")
    81→        Component(embed_pipeline, "Embedding Pipeline", "Vector generation for semantic search")
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    Rel(obj_extract, obj_crud, "Creates objective via")
    85→    Rel(alignment_eval, supports_writer, "Creates edge when similarity >= 0.7")
    86→    Rel(beh_collector, beh_scorer, "Normalizes raw telemetry into 0-1 score")
    87→    Rel(beh_query, policy_gate, "Provides behavior scores for context enrichment")
    88→    Rel(coherence_scan, observer_diag, "Feeds disconnected patterns into existing observation pipeline")
    89→    Rel(beh_learning, observer_diag, "Feeds behavior trends into existing clustering + root cause pipeline")
    90→    Rel(beh_learning, learning_api, "Proposes learnings via existing API")
    91→    Rel(obj_crud, embed_pipeline, "Generates embeddings for objectives")
    92→    Rel(alignment_eval, embed_pipeline, "Generates intent goal embedding")
    93→```
    94→
    95→---
    96→
    97→## Integration Points
    98→
    99→### 1. Extraction Pipeline Extension (Objective Extraction)
   100→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/extraction/` processes chat messages, extracts task/decision/question entities
   101→- **Extension**: Add `objective` to extraction entity types. Reuse existing extraction schema pattern with confidence scoring and dedup
   102→- **Dedup**: Semantic similarity check (>0.95) against active workspace objectives before creation
   103→
   104→### 2. Authorizer Extension (Objective Alignment)
   105→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts` runs policy gate then LLM evaluation
   106→- **Extension**: After policy gate passes, run alignment evaluation: KNN query against active objective embeddings. Create `supports` edge on match (>=0.7), warning observation on no match (<0.5 for all)
   107→- **Constraint**: Alignment evaluation must complete within 200ms. Use pre-loaded workspace objective embeddings (cache in memory per scan cycle, or two-step KNN)
   108→
   109→### 3. Policy Gate Extension (Behavior Scores in Context)
   110→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/policy/policy-gate.ts` evaluates rules against `IntentEvaluationContext`
   111→- **Extension**: Enrich `IntentEvaluationContext` with `behavior_scores` object (keyed by metric_type) before policy evaluation. Predicate evaluator already supports dot-path resolution (`behavior_scores.Security_First` with operator `lt` and value `0.8`)
   112→- **No schema change to policy rules needed**: The existing `RulePredicate` type already supports `field: "behavior_scores.Security_First", operator: "lt", value: 0.8` -- the predicate evaluator resolves dot paths
   113→
   114→### 4. Observer Extension (Behavior Telemetry + Coherence)
   115→- **Existing**: `app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts` runs contradiction/stale/drift scans. `learning-diagnosis.ts` clusters observations and proposes learnings
   116→- **Extension A (Telemetry)**: New scan type in graph-scan that queries recent agent sessions and writes behavior records via behavior collector
   117→- **Extension B (Coherence)**: New scan type that detects orphaned decisions (no implementing task after 14d), stale objectives (no supports edges after 14d)
   118→- **Extension C (Behavior Learning)**: New input signal type for learning-diagnosis that feeds behavior trend data (3+ consecutive below-threshold) into existing clustering + root cause pipeline
   119→
   120→### 5. Feed Integration
   121→- **Existing**: SSE events via `app/src/server/streaming/sse-registry.ts`
   122→- **Extension**: New feed card types for alignment warnings, behavior vetos, coherence alerts. Reuse existing observation + suggestion feed card patterns
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Data Flow Diagrams
   127→
   128→### Intent Authorization with Objective Alignment
   129→
   130→```
   131→Intent Submitted
   132→  |
   133→  v
   134→[Policy Gate] -- evaluates rules against context (now includes behavior_scores)
   135→  |
   136→  |-- DENY --> veto (existing flow)
   137→  |
   138→  v (PASS)
   139→[Alignment Evaluator] -- KNN: intent.goal embedding vs active objective embeddings
   140→  |
   141→  |-- similarity >= 0.7 --> create supports edge, proceed
   142→  |-- 0.5 <= similarity < 0.7 --> create supports edge (highest), surface for confirmation
   143→  |-- similarity < 0.5 for all --> create warning observation, proceed (warning mode)
   144→  |-- no objectives exist --> skip alignment, proceed with info feed card
   145→  |
   146→  v
   147→[LLM Evaluator] -- existing flow (unchanged)
   148→  |
   149→  v
   150→Authorization Result
   151→```
   152→
   153→### Behavior Telemetry Pipeline
   154→
   155→```
   156→Agent Session Completes
   157→  |
   158→  v
   159→[Observer Graph Scan] -- queries recent sessions without behavior records
   160→  |
   161→  v
   162→[Behavior Collector] -- for each session:
   163→  |
   164→  |-- extract session telemetry (files_changed, test files, etc.)
   165→  |-- [Behavior Scorer] -- metric-specific normalization (pure function)
   166→  |-- write behavior record to SurrealDB
   167→  |-- create exhibits edge: identity ->exhibits-> behavior
   168→  |
   169→  v
   170→[Behavior Trend Check] -- queries last N behavior records per identity+metric
   171→  |
   172→  |-- 3+ consecutive below threshold --> feed into learning proposal pipeline
   173→  |-- improvement detected after learning --> create positive observation
   174→  |-- no improvement after N sessions --> create warning observation
   175→```
   176→
   177→### Coherence Audit Pipeline
   178→
   179→```
   180→[Observer Graph Scan] -- periodic trigger (extends existing scan)
   181→  |
   182→  +-- [Query Orphaned Decisions] -- confirmed decisions with no implementing task after 14d
   183→  |     |
   184→  |     v create observation (severity: warning)
   185→  |
   186→  +-- [Query Stale Objectives] -- active objectives with no supports edge after 14d
   187→  |     |
   188→  |     v create observation (severity: warning)
   189→  |
   190→  +-- [Query Disconnected Tasks] -- tasks with no project/feature after 14d
   191→  |     |
   192→  |     v create observation (severity: info)
   193→  |
   194→  +-- [Compute Coherence Score] -- connected_nodes / total_nodes
   195→        |
   196→        v store on workspace or emit as feed event
   197→```
   198→
   199→---
   200→
   201→## Quality Attribute Strategies
   202→
   203→### Auditability
   204→- Behavior records are append-only (no UPDATE, no retroactive modification)
   205→- `supports` edges are immutable once created
   206→- Objective status changes logged with timestamp (created_at/updated_at)
   207→- Policy veto overrides create observation with identity reference
   208→- All behavior-driven learnings linked to triggering behavior records via `learning_evidence`
   209→
   210→### Maintainability
   211→- New behavior metric types added by extending `metric_type` enum in schema migration -- no code changes needed beyond scorer function
   212→- Policy behavior rules use existing predicate evaluator with dot-path resolution -- no policy schema changes
   213→- Coherence queries are pure SurrealQL -- new disconnected patterns added as queries, not code
   214→
   215→### Testability
   216→- Behavior scorer is a pure function (input: session telemetry -> output: normalized score)
   217→- Alignment evaluator is a pure function (input: intent embedding + objective embeddings -> output: alignment result)
   218→- Coherence scanner is pure graph queries against test DB
   219→- All existing acceptance test infrastructure (in-process server + isolated DB) applies
   220→
   221→### Performance
   222→- Alignment evaluation: two-step KNN pattern (HNSW index for candidates, then filter by workspace). Pre-limit to 20 candidates
   223→- Behavior score lookup: indexed query on `workspace + identity + metric_type`, ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
   224→- Coherence scan: bounded by LIMIT clauses (50 per entity type), runs in background (not request path)
   225→
   226→### Reliability
   227→- Behavior scoring failure does NOT block agent sessions (fire-and-forget via inflight tracker)
   228→- Alignment failure does NOT block intent authorization in warning mode (fallback: proceed without alignment)
   229→- Coherence audit failures logged as observations (existing pattern from graph-scan.ts error handling)
   230→
   231→---
   232→
   233→## Deployment Architecture
   234→
   235→No changes to deployment topology. Single Bun server process, single SurrealDB instance. New tables and indexes added via versioned migration scripts applied with `bun migrate`.
   236→
   237→Migration sequence:
   238→1. `0032_objective_table.surql` -- objective table + has_objective relation + supports relation + observes extension
   239→2. `0033_behavior_table.surql` -- behavior table + exhibits relation
   240→3. `0034_fulltext_objective.surql` -- fulltext search index for objectives (optional, if entity search needs it)
   241→
   242→---
   243→
   244→## Technology Stack
   245→
   246→See `docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/technology-stack.md` for rationale.
   247→
   248→## Component Boundaries
   249→
   250→See `docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/component-boundaries.md` for module decomposition.
   251→
   252→## Data Models
   253→
   254→See `docs/feature/objective-behavior/design/data-models.md` for SurrealDB schema design.
   255→

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}
